result.scope: Download orchestration and persisted download records (MuseAmp/Backend/Downloads)
result.summary: This scope owns the entire download pipeline for remote songs: queueing, URL resolution, transfer via the Digger library, network-aware deferral, post-download finalization (artwork/lyrics/metadata embedding), library ingestion, and crash-safe persistence. The central type is `DownloadManager`, a `@MainActor` class split across five files (core + `+Queue`, `+Digger`, `+Network`, `+Persistence` extensions); it owns all in-memory download state in a `tasks: [String: ActiveDownloadTask]` dictionary keyed by trackID, exposes it to UI via a Combine `CurrentValueSubject` (`tasksPublisher`), and is constructed once in `AppEnvironment` (no singleton). `DownloadStore` is a thin, non-throwing facade over `MusicLibraryDatabase` (MuseAmpDatabaseKit/WCDB) that persists `DownloadJob` records for in-flight work only — completed downloads delete their record and the track lands in the library tracks table; "is downloaded" is derived from the tracks table plus on-disk file existence, never from a stored flag. The task lifecycle is a state machine (`waiting -> resolving -> downloading -> finalizing`, with `waitingForNetwork`, `paused`, `failed` branches), with every state transition mirrored to the database via `persistRecord` so that `reconcileOnLaunch` (called from SceneDelegate) can rehydrate interrupted/queued/finalizing/failed jobs after a relaunch. Finalization moves the Digger cache file to a `.tmp.`-prefixed staging file, concurrently fetches artwork (`DownloadArtworkProcessor`) and lyrics (`DownloadLyricsProcessor`), embeds metadata via `ExportMetadataProcessor` (AVAssetExportSession passthrough re-export including a JSON comment carrying trackID/albumID), then hands the file to `DatabaseManager.send(.ingestAudioFile)` for library ingestion. `NetworkMonitor` (NWPathMonitor wrapper) drives WiFi/cellular/none transitions; cellular downloads are deferred per-track unless explicitly allowed via `allowCellularDownload`. `ExportMetadataProcessor` is also reused outside this scope by `Backend/Lyrics/LyricsReloadService` and `Backend/Sync/SyncPreparedTrackBuilder+Export`, so its embed/verify contract crosses feature boundaries.
conventions: Responsibility-based file splits on one type: DownloadManager.swift (state + public API) plus DownloadManager+Queue.swift (scheduling/rehydration), +Digger.swift (transfer callbacks/finalization kickoff), +Network.swift (connectivity observation), +Persistence.swift (DB mirroring/cleanup helpers); all extension members are internal so extensions share manager state directly

Stateless post-processing helpers are caseless enums with static funcs (DownloadArtworkProcessor, DownloadLyricsProcessor, ExportMetadataProcessor) and a private `static let logger = "TypeName"` string passed to AppLog

Concurrency: DownloadManager and NetworkMonitor are @MainActor; value types are explicitly marked `nonisolated struct`; DownloadStore is `final nonisolated class`; Digger callbacks hop back via DispatchQueue.main.async; background work uses Task(priority: .utility) with [weak self] and re-enters the actor via `await self?.completeFinalization(...)`

Timeout/once patterns use shared helpers from Backend/Supplement/ConcurrencyHelpers (OnceGuard, UncheckedSendableBox) wrapped in private `_prefixed` callback-style funcs bridged with withCheckedThrowingContinuation

Guard-and-early-return everywhere: `guard let task = tasks[trackID] else { AppLog.warning(...); return }` is the standard preamble; mutations go through `tasks[trackID]?.field = ...` optional-chained subscript writes

Logging: every state transition logs AppLog.info with key=value formatting (trackID=, error=, count=); every swallowed catch logs AppLog.error or .warning; file I/O successes log .verbose — consistent with repo-wide Logging Rules
DownloadStore methods never throw: each wraps the database call in do/catch, logs via AppLog.error, and returns a neutral default ([], nil, false, 0)

Persistence mirroring convention: every in-memory state change is immediately followed by persistRecord(trackID:state:...) which rebuilds a full DownloadJob from the live task and upserts it (DownloadJobStatus is the persisted enum, ActiveDownloadTask.State the in-memory one — they are mapped, not shared)

UI publish throttling: scheduleProgressPublish coalesces progress/speed updates to >=0.2s intervals via a pending DispatchWorkItem; publishSnapshot cancels the pending item, sorts failed tasks last then by allocated queueOrder
Trailing commas in multi-line argument lists and Swift 5.9 switch-expression syntax (`let x = switch ... { ... }`) are used consistently
File staging naming: finalization temp files are `.tmp.<filename>` siblings (Self.finalizingURL), export temps are UUID-named siblings replaced atomically via FileManager.replaceItemAt
dataFlows: Submission: UI (AlbumDetailViewController, PlaylistDetailViewController+Actions) builds [SongDownloadRequest] and calls downloadManager.submitRequests via AppEnvironment; the manager dedupes against in-memory tasks, the tracks table (downloadStore.isDownloaded = track row + file exists on disk), and existing DownloadJob records (failed records are deleted and re-queued), then creates a DownloadJob (jobID = UUID) and an ActiveDownloadTask; destination path is sanitizePathComponent(albumID)/sanitizePathComponent(trackID).m4a

Dual-state invariant: every ActiveDownloadTask mutation that changes lifecycle state is mirrored to the DB through persistRecord -> downloadStore.upsert -> MusicLibraryDatabase (WCDB, MuseAmpDatabaseKit); the DB row is rebuilt wholesale from the in-memory task each time, so the in-memory dictionary is the source of truth while running and the DB is the crash-recovery journal. Note persistRecord defaults progress to 0 unless explicitly passed, so persisted progress is coarse (0, first-progress fraction, or 1 at finalizing)

URL resolution crosses to Backend/API: startResolving calls apiClient.playback(id:) (async) to obtain the source URL, which is then stored on the task and in DownloadJob.sourceURL; resolution failures mark the task .failed with the error string persisted

Transfer: Digger (third-party) downloads to its own cache; on completion the cached file is moved to a .tmp.<trackID>.m4a staging sibling of the final audio path (paths.absoluteAudioURL(for: destinationRelativePath), LibraryPaths from MuseAmpDatabaseKit); NSURLErrorCancelled completions are silently dropped if intentionallyPaused, auto-requeued up to 3 retries otherwise

Finalization fan-out (Task priority .utility, off-actor): DownloadArtworkProcessor fetches artwork (disk-cached at locations.artworkCacheURL(for:), URLSession direct — not Kingfisher/APIClient) and embeds it via AVAssetExportSession passthrough re-export; DownloadLyricsProcessor fetches via apiClient.lyrics(id:) and persists through LyricsCacheStore; ExportMetadataProcessor re-exports the file embedding an iTunes user-comment JSON {v, trackID, albumID, artworkURL} plus title/artist/album/lyrics items — metadata embed failure is logged as warning but does NOT fail the download

Ingestion handoff: the staged .tmp file plus ImportedTrackMetadata (sourceKind: .downloaded) is sent to databaseManager.send(.ingestAudioFile) in MuseAmpDatabaseKit; on success the DownloadJob record is DELETED and the in-memory task removed — downloaded-ness is thereafter derived from the tracks table + file existence (no persistent download record for completed items); on ingest failure the task is marked .failed with progress 1 and the record kept

Crash recovery: SceneDelegate calls reconcileOnLaunch; downloading/resolving records are rewritten as .queued and rehydrated as waiting; .finalizing records resume finalization from the .tmp. or final file if present (else requeued with progress reset); legacy records with empty targetRelativePath get a path re-inferred via paths.inferredRelativePath and the record is rewritten — a data-migration write path worth auditing

Network gating: NetworkMonitor (NWPathMonitor) publishes wifi/cellular/none; cellular/none transitions stop active Digger tasks, mark them intentionallyPaused, and persist .waitingForNetwork; per-track cellular consent lives only in the in-memory cellularAllowedTrackIDs set (not persisted — lost on relaunch); wiredEthernet and unknown-satisfied paths are treated as wifi

Failure cleanup: cleanupLocalAudioArtifacts deletes both the .tmp. staging file and the final audio file for failed/cancelled/removed tasks — it deletes finalURL unconditionally for the task's destination path, which is the path also used by completed library tracks (same album/track-derived path scheme)

ExportMetadataProcessor and DownloadArtworkProcessor helpers (resolveOutputFileType, temporaryOutputURL, artworkMetadataItems, collectMetadataItems, export) are shared dependencies of Backend/Lyrics/LyricsReloadService and Backend/Sync/SyncPreparedTrackBuilder+Export, so changes to the embedded comment JSON schema or validation (isCatalogID checks in validateExportInfo/verifyEmbeddedMetadata) affect lyrics reload and LAN transfer integrity as well; note the download finalization path calls embedExportMetadata WITHOUT validateExportInfo, while the Sync path validates first

UI observation: tasksPublisher (CurrentValueSubject<[ActiveDownloadTask], Never>) feeds Downloads browse UI, album detail, settings, and TabBarController badges; snapshots are throttled to 5 Hz during progress; screenAwakeHandler (injected MainActor closure from AppEnvironment) toggles idle-timer based on whether any task is resolving/downloading/finalizing

result.scope: PlaybackController bridge + app-facing media models (MuseAmp/Backend/Playback, MuseAmp/Backend/Models)
result.summary: MuseAmp/Backend/Playback owns all app-level playback state. The central type is PlaybackController (@MainActor final class, ObservableObject, 553 lines plus four responsibility extensions), which wraps MuseAmpPlayerKit.MusicPlayer, acts as its MusicPlayerDelegate, and is the single source of truth UI observes via the @Published `snapshot` (PlaybackSnapshot) and the high-frequency `playbackTimeSubject`. It is created once in AppEnvironment (iOS) and TVAppContext (tvOS) — every Playback file is mirrored into MuseAmpTV/Backend/Playback via relative symlinks, so this scope compiles in both targets. Internal mutable state is grouped into nested nonisolated structs (QueueState with currentSource/trackLookup/itemCache, RestoreState, SeekState). Supporting value types are PlaybackTrack (the app's canonical track model), PlaybackSnapshot (immutable derived view with computed currentTrack/history/upcoming), PlaybackSource (custom-Codable enum tagging where a queue came from), PlaybackOutputDevice (AVAudioSession route mapping), and PlaybackSessionStore (JSON file persistence of PersistedPlaybackSession/PersistedPlaybackTrack for queue restore across launches). PlaybackController+Resolution.swift is the local-file-only URL resolution layer: it maps PlaybackTracks to PlayerItems by probing track.localFileURL, then database.allTrackRelativePaths() under paths.audioDirectory, then a cached item, and throws PlaybackResolutionError.localFileUnavailable otherwise — there is no remote streaming fallback. Queue PlayerItems get synthetic IDs ("queue-item|UUID|trackID") built/parsed by makeQueueItemID/sourceTrackID so duplicate tracks can coexist in a queue. MuseAmp/Backend/Models is a thin adapter layer: five small extension files that convert between SubsonicClientKit's CatalogSong, MuseAmpDatabaseKit's PlaylistEntry/AudioTrackRecord, PlaybackTrack, and MuseAmpInterfaceKit cell-content structs (SongRowContent, AlbumTrackCellContent, AMNowPlayingQueueTrackPresenting), plus SongExportItem, the value type carrying source URL + sanitized "Artist - Title" base filename for export/share/sync flows (not mirrored to tvOS, which has its own TV* models).
conventions: Controller split by responsibility: PlaybackController.swift (core API + lifecycle) plus +Delegate, +Snapshot, +Resolution, +Logging extension files; same split mandated by AGENTS.md.
Mutable controller state grouped into nested nonisolated structs (QueueState, RestoreState, SeekState) accessed as queueState/restoreState/seekState properties rather than loose stored properties.
Value types are declared `nonisolated struct`/`nonisolated enum` throughout; NSObjectProtocol observers and Timer are `nonisolated(unsafe)` vars cleaned up in deinit.
Heavy guard/early-return style: nearly every mutating method opens with `guard !tracks.isEmpty else { return ... }` or an index-containment guard.

All diagnostics through AppLog with key=value formatting (e.g. "trackID=... state=..."); log-text helpers `string(for:)` overloads and `sanitizedLogText`/`formattedPlaybackTime` keep enum/state names and user text log-safe; every catch logs via AppLog.error before returning nil/empty.
Static factory helpers prefixed make- (makePlayerItem, makeQueuedPlayerItem, makeQueueItemID, makePersistedSession, makePersistedTrack); resolution helpers are nonisolated static where possible.

Models files named `TargetType+AppModels.swift` and contain only conversion initializers/computed adapters — no stored state; conversions take APIClient (or apiBaseURL) as a parameter to resolve artwork via mediaURL(from:width:height:) at the standard 600x600.
Async closures bridging from sync contexts use `Task { @MainActor [weak self] in }`; delegate callbacks funnel into refreshSnapshot(persistState:) rather than mutating snapshot fields directly.

Snapshot publishing distinguishes `latestSnapshot` (always current, internal reads) from @Published `snapshot` (skipped when isUIPublishingSuspended); lightweight vs full refresh path chosen by canPerformLightweightSnapshotRefresh.
Trailing commas in multi-line argument lists and 4-space indentation everywhere; free functions allowed for pure formatting (formattedPlaybackTime/formattedDuration in PlaybackTimeFormatting.swift).
dataFlows: Queue intake: UI calls play/playNext/addToQueue with [PlaybackTrack]; resolvePlayableItems (concurrent TaskGroup, order restored by originalIndex sort) converts to PlayerItems via local-file probing — track.localFileURL, then database.allTrackRelativePaths() joined to paths.audioDirectory, then queueState.itemCache; unresolvable tracks are silently dropped from the queue (logged at error), shifting indices, which preferredStartIndex/restoredCurrentIndex compensate for via originalIndex.

Identity mapping: queue PlayerItem IDs are synthetic ('queue-item|<UUID>|<trackID>'); sourceTrackID() parses them back and queueState.trackLookup maps item.id -> PlaybackTrack. track(for:) falls back to reconstructing a PlaybackTrack from PlayerItem fields if lookup misses (loses albumID/localFileURL in that path — relevant for persistence fidelity).

State publication: MusicPlayerDelegate callbacks -> refreshSnapshot() -> builds PlaybackSnapshot from player.queue + queueState + playlistStore.isLiked + AVAudioSession route; writes latestSnapshot always, @Published snapshot only when !isUIPublishingSuspended; high-frequency time goes through playbackTimeSubject and latestSnapshot.withTime, never the publisher. Pending-seek time (seekState.pendingSeekSnapshotTime) overrides player.currentTime while paused/idle to avoid stale-time flicker.

Persistence: makePersistedSession() snapshots ordered queue, currentTrackID/index/time, shuffle/repeat/source/shouldResumePlayback into PersistedPlaybackSession; PlaybackSessionStore writes pretty-printed JSON atomically to paths.playbackStateURL, with createDirectory-before-write and clear() on unrecoverable restore. persistState:true is threaded through nearly every mutation; SceneDelegate/TVSceneDelegate also call persistPlaybackState/setUIPublishingSuspended on lifecycle events.

Restore: restorePersistedPlaybackIfNeeded (one-shot via restoreState.didAttemptPersistedRestore) rebuilds PlaybackTracks from persisted records — local audio re-resolved from localRelativePath via paths.absoluteAudioURL; artwork restored preferring remote URL string, then artwork cache file, then re-extracting embedded artwork from the audio file via metadataReader and writing it back to paths.artworkCacheURL (a write side-effect during restore); local file artwork URLs are never persisted (persistedArtworkURLString returns nil for file URLs) and stale local paths are dropped.

Cross-layer boundaries crossed by Playback: MuseAmpPlayerKit (player engine), MuseAmpDatabaseKit (MusicLibraryDatabase track paths, PlaylistStore liked-state, LibraryPaths, EmbeddedMetadataReader), AVFoundation (route observation), NotificationCenter (.playlistsDidChange -> snapshot refresh), and disk (session JSON + rebuilt artwork files). Liked toggles flow PlaybackController -> playlistStore.toggleLiked(track.playlistEntry) -> snapshot refresh; the lock-screen Like command is wired in init.

Models layer transforms: CatalogSong (SubsonicClientKit) -> PlaybackTrack / PlaylistEntry / SongDownloadRequest; PlaylistEntry & AudioTrackRecord -> SongRowContent (display strings, ' · ' joined subtitle, sanitizedTrackTitle); PlaylistEntry -> SongDownloadRequest via static APIClient.resolveMediaURL with apiBaseURL. Duration units convert between millis (Catalog/PlaylistEntry) and seconds (PlaybackTrack/PlayerItem) at these boundaries — playlistEntry computed property on PlaybackTrack rounds seconds*1000 back to millis.

SongExportItem is constructed by menu providers/export presenters and Sync's SyncPreparedTrackBuilder from local file URLs; it derives preferredFileBaseName ('Artist - Title') through sanitizeDisplayFileName with 'Unknown' fallback — file-naming integrity for exports/AirDrop/LAN transfer flows lives here.
Target sharing: all 11 Playback files are symlinked into MuseAmpTV/Backend/Playback (any new Playback file needs a matching symlink); Backend/Models is iOS-only — tvOS keeps separate TV* model files.

result.summary: This scope holds two small app-target backend subdomains, both constructed and owned by AppEnvironment (MuseAmp/Application/AppEnvironment.swift) and both delegating actual persistence to MuseAmpDatabaseKit. The Playlist side centers on PlaylistStore, a non-throwing facade over DatabaseManager: every mutation (create/import/delete/rename/cover/addSong/removeSong/move/duplicate/clear/updateLyrics) is expressed as a LibraryCommand sent via databaseManager.sendSynchronously, after which the store re-fetches its in-memory `playlists: [Playlist]` cache and posts `.playlistsDidChange` ("amusic.playlistsDidChange") only when an Equatable diff against the pre-mutation snapshot shows change. It special-cases a sentinel zero-UUID "Liked Songs" playlist (Playlist.swift extension): auto-created at init with a generated heart PNG (LikedSongsPlaylistArtwork), name re-synced to the current locale, metadata backfilled, and auto-deleted when its last song is removed. PlaylistCoverArtworkCache is an actor that renders NxN mosaic covers from entry artwork via Kingfisher, caching in an NSCache plus PNG files under Caches/PlaylistCoverArtworkCache keyed by "v3-{id}-{updatedAt ms}-{songCount}-{sidePixels}", and posting `.playlistArtworkDidUpdate`. PlaylistTransferDocument is the versioned Codable .musiclist export/import format (drops entryID/artworkURL/lyrics; tolerates a legacy `entries` JSON key). The Lyrics side has LyricsService (cache-first read: LyricsCacheStore, then APIClient.lyrics, persisting only for locally downloaded tracks), LyricsReloadService (heavier path: prefer lyrics embedded in the downloaded M4A via AVFoundation, else fetch remote, persist to cache, re-embed into the audio file via ExportMetadataProcessor, post `.lyricsDidUpdate`; also a destructive rebuildAllLyricsIndex that wipes the whole cache then refetches every track sequentially), and LyricsChineseScriptConverter, a pure CFStringTransform Hans/Hant presentation utility with no persistence. UI layers (Interface/Playlist, Interface/NowPlaying, Interface/Sync, Interface/Root) consume these services through AppEnvironment and observe via NotificationCenter and the DatabaseKit events publisher.
conventions: Responsibility-based file splits: PlaylistStore.swift (CRUD entry points) + PlaylistStore+Support.swift (event binding, Liked Songs helpers, mutation plumbing) + PlaylistStore+Bridge.swift — note Bridge is currently an empty stub containing only imports

Canonical mutation pattern: snapshot `previousPlaylists`, run throwing `send(LibraryCommand)` inside do/catch that only AppLog.error's (errors never propagate to callers), then `reload()` + `notifyIfNeeded(previousPlaylists:)`; centralized in `performMutation(action:previousPlaylists:operation:)` and `finishMutation(previousPlaylists:pruneLikedPlaylistIfNeededFor:)`

Change notification is diff-driven: NotificationCenter post only when `playlists != previousPlaylists` (relies on Playlist Equatable); notification names namespaced `amusic.*` with userInfo keys via AppNotificationUserInfoKey

Logging via AppLog with structured key=value message strings (trackID=, playlistID=, error=); levels follow project rules — .verbose for cache hits, .info for state transitions/renders/network success, .warning for degraded results, .error for failures; every swallowed catch logs
Caseless `enum` namespaces for pure static utilities (LikedSongsPlaylistArtwork, LyricsChineseScriptConverter, PlaylistTransferFileType)

Swift 6 isolation style: `nonisolated` annotations on value types/enums/extensions (PlaylistTransferDocument, LikedToggleResult, LyricsChineseScriptConverter); PlaylistCoverArtworkCache is an `actor` with @Sendable closure parameters
guard-based early returns throughout; @discardableResult on mutators that return best-effort values (e.g. `playlist(for: id) ?? candidate`)
FileManager.default used directly inline (never injected), per project rules; trailing commas in multi-line argument lists; 4-space indent
Sentinel-ID pattern for the Liked Songs system playlist (all-zero UUID constant on the DatabaseKit Playlist type) rather than a stored flag

App-side extension file (Playlist.swift) adapts the package model: `songs` is a get/set alias for `entries`; the store re-wraps incoming PlaylistEntry values field-by-field before sending to drop caller-supplied entryIDs
dataFlows: Playlist write path: UI -> PlaylistStore method -> LibraryCommand -> DatabaseManager.sendSynchronously (MuseAmpDatabaseKit / WCDB owns durable state) -> reload() via databaseManager.fetchPlaylists() -> in-memory `playlists` array -> diff -> `.playlistsDidChange`. The in-memory array is a read cache, never the source of truth; all command errors are swallowed and state is re-synced from DB regardless
createPlaylist and importPlaylist both reuse the `.importLegacyPlaylists([candidate])` command for inserts

updateSong(in:at:with:) is non-atomic: it sends `.clearPlaylistEntries` then re-adds every entry with individual `.addPlaylistEntry` commands — a failure mid-loop can persist a truncated playlist (integrity-sensitive)

refreshSongs(in:using:) fans out concurrent APIClient.song(id:) fetches in a task group, merges remote fields into existing entries by trackID (preserving entryID and lyrics, keeping old albumID/title/duration/trackNumber when remote is nil but unconditionally overwriting artworkURL), then calls index-based updateSong per changed row — each of which is itself a full clear+rewrite of the playlist

Liked Songs lifecycle: ensureLikedSongsPlaylist creates the zero-UUID playlist with LikedSongsPlaylistArtwork.makePNGData() at PlaylistStore init; syncLikedSongsNameToCurrentLocale renames it on every launch if it differs from the localized default; finishMutation deletes the entire playlist when its last entry is removed (toggleLiked/removeSong/clearSongs paths)
Inbound external changes: databaseManager.events (Combine) -> receive on main -> reload + diff-notify, covering writes made outside PlaylistStore (e.g. sync/transfer, other DatabaseManager clients)

Cover artwork flow: playlist entries' artworkURL template -> @Sendable urlResolver closure supplied by callers (resolved through APIClient.mediaURL per project rules) -> KingfisherManager image retrieval -> UIGraphicsImageRenderer mosaic -> NSCache (countLimit 512) + atomic PNG write to Caches/PlaylistCoverArtworkCache. Cache key embeds playlist updatedAt + song count, so stale covers orphan on disk rather than being overwritten; invalidateCache removes ALL memory objects but only prefix-matched files. Note: shuffled renders skip cache reads and the artwork-did-update notification but still write memory+disk under the same deterministic key, so a randomized mosaic can replace the canonical cached cover (integrity-sensitive)

Playlist export/import: Playlist -> PlaylistTransferDocument (version 1; strips entryID, artworkURL, per-entry lyrics; keeps coverImageData) -> JSON .musiclist file (UTType wiki.qaq.museamp.musiclist), consumed by MuseAmp/Interface/Playlist/PlaylistTransferCoordinator.swift which round-trips through PlaylistStore.importPlaylist; decoder accepts the legacy `entries` key and missing version/exportedAt

Lyrics read path: LyricsService.loadLyrics — LyricsCacheStore (MuseAmpDatabaseKit, file/DB-backed) first, else APIClient.lyrics(id:) over SubsonicClientKit; fetched lyrics persist to the cache only when MusicLibraryDatabase.hasTrack confirms the track is downloaded locally; per-entry playlist lyrics are separately persisted via PlaylistStore.updateLyrics (.updateEntryLyrics command)

Lyrics reload path (LyricsReloadService.reloadLyrics): resolves the downloaded file via LibraryPaths.absoluteAudioURL; prefers lyrics embedded in M4A metadata (AVURLAsset + AVMetadataHelper) -> cache; otherwise network fetch -> cache -> re-embeds lyrics INTO the on-disk audio file via ExportMetadataProcessor.embedExportMetadata (mutates user files; embed failure is logged as warning and tolerated) -> posts `.lyricsDidUpdate` (defined in MuseAmp/Backend/Library/LibraryChangeNotifications.swift)

rebuildAllLyricsIndex is destructive-then-rebuild: lyricsCacheStore.removeAllLyrics() up front, then sequential forceRemoteFetch per track with per-track error tolerance — interruption or failures leave the lyrics cache partially populated
LyricsChineseScriptConverter performs locale-driven Hans<->Hant CFStringTransform at display time only; converted text is never written back to cache, DB, or files

result.scope: APIClient + local/remote library providers, importer, metadata reader (MuseAmp/Backend/API, MuseAmp/Backend/Library)
result.summary: Backend/API holds APIClient, the single app-level network orchestration entry point: a final @unchecked Sendable class wrapping SubsonicClientKit's SubsonicMusicService behind intent-level async methods (ping, searchSongs/searchAlbums, album, song, lyrics, playback) plus artwork URL template resolution (resolveMediaURL handles {w}/{h} placeholders, protocol-relative //, scheme-less relative URLs against baseURL). Its mutable configuration (baseURL/username/password) is NSLock-guarded and lazily re-read from AppPreferences.currentSubsonicConfiguration on every synchronizedState() call, hot-rebuilding the underlying service when server settings change; APIClient+Transfer.swift extends it with the LAN sync-transfer HTTP surface (POST /auth bearer-token handshake, GET /manifest with protocol-version gate, GET /track/{id} download with cleanup-on-failure) speaking Backend/Sync JSON models. Backend/Library is the thin bridge between app code and MuseAmpDatabaseKit's DatabaseManager: MusicLibraryDatabase (core + +Tracks/+Downloads/+Ingest/+Bridge extensions) routes reads through synchronous DatabaseManager queries and writes through LibraryCommand (sendSynchronouslyIfSupported first, async send fallback); +Bridge.swift is currently an empty placeholder. AudioFileImporter is the file-picker import pipeline: security-scoped access held for the whole batch, recursive audio discovery, AVAsset metadata inspection, mandatory catalog trackID/albumID extraction from embedded JSON comment (files without it are rejected as noMetadata), three-layer duplicate detection (intra-batch set, existing DB records via case-insensitive title/artist/album + 2s duration tolerance, destination-file existence), staging copy into paths.incomingDirectory, then database.ingestAudioFile to commit, with optional detached-Task background artwork fetch. EmbeddedMetadataReader extracts AVFoundation metadata (title/artist/album/track/disc/genre/composer/releaseDate/lyrics/artwork presence) into AudioTrackRecord values with localized Unknown Artist/Album fallbacks. AudioTrackRecord+AppModels is the adapter seam converting DB records into PlaylistEntry, CatalogSong, PlaybackTrack, SongExportItem, and ImportedTrackMetadata for the Playback/Playlist/Sync/Interface layers. SongLibraryIndexer wraps the .rebuildIndex command; MusicLibraryTrackRemovalService loops .removeTrack synchronously (swallowing errors after logging); TrackArtworkRepairService re-resolves artwork (embedded comment JSON, then song lookup, then album lookup via APIClient at 600x600), caches it atomically, embeds it into the audio file via DownloadArtworkProcessor, and posts .artworkDidUpdate. State ownership: AppEnvironment constructs all these as let properties; persisted state lives in DatabaseManager (WCDB inside MuseAmpDatabaseKit); this scope keeps no caches beyond APIClient's lock-guarded config.
conventions: final class + @unchecked Sendable with explicit nonisolated members/extensions everywhere; nonisolated(unsafe) vars only behind an NSLock, accessed via stateLock.withLock, with a Locked suffix on methods that assume the lock is held (refreshConfigurationIfNeededLocked, rebuildServiceLocked)

Responsibility-split extension files named TypeName+Responsibility.swift (APIClient+Transfer, MusicLibraryDatabase+Tracks/+Downloads/+Ingest/+Bridge); core file stays minimal (MusicLibraryDatabase.swift is just init + stored deps)
private extension TypeName blocks at the bottom of each file for helpers and nested result types (SingleImportResult, ImportedTrack, DuplicateKey, EmbeddedCatalogIDs)

AppLog tracing pattern on every network/IO entry point: .verbose on entry with parameters, .info on success with result metrics, .error on failure then rethrow via do/catch wrap; try? paths that swallow errors log AppLog.warning/.error first; sensitive values not logged (sanitizedLogText caps response bodies at 120 chars)
Domain-specific error enums per service (SyncTransferError, RepairError: LocalizedError with errorDescription); one legacy NSError(domain:code:) fallback in +Ingest
guard/early-return heavy control flow; Swift trailing-comma style after the last argument in multi-line calls and parameter lists
Progress callbacks typed (@MainActor (...) -> Void)? for UI consumers or @Sendable for indexer; NotificationCenter posts wrapped in await MainActor.run
FileManager.default used directly inline (project rule: never injected or stored)
Adapter conversions live as computed properties / methods in a Record+AppModels.swift extension file rather than mapper types

Names: *Service for stateless operations (TrackArtworkRepairService, MusicLibraryTrackRemovalService), *Indexer/*Importer/*Reader for pipelines, OrNil suffix for non-throwing lookup variants (trackOrNil)
dataFlows: Remote catalog reads: UI -> APIClient -> SubsonicMusicService -> CatalogSong/CatalogAlbum/PlaybackInfo; APIClient.playback rewrites relative playbackURL to absolute against the current baseURL by reconstructing PlaybackInfo field-by-field (MuseAmp/Backend/API/APIClient.swift:142-166)

Server configuration: AppPreferences.currentSubsonicConfiguration (ConfigurableKit/UserDefaults) is re-resolved inside the lock on every request via synchronizedState(); a change in baseURL/username/password silently rebuilds SubsonicMusicService mid-flight, so in-progress calls may straddle two configurations

LAN transfer download: APIClient+Transfer buffers the entire track body as Data in memory (no streaming), pre-creates the destination file, overwrites any existing file at destinationURL, writes via data.write(to:), deletes the partial file in the catch path, and reports progress only as a single 1.0 callback (MuseAmp/Backend/API/APIClient+Transfer.swift:84-137)

Import pipeline (creates persisted records): picker URLs -> discoverAudioFiles -> AVAsset metadata -> catalog trackID/albumID parsed from embedded JSON comment (iTunesMetadataUserComment); validated only by String.isCatalogID -> duplicate gates (intra-batch DuplicateKey set with 2s duration buckets; existingTracks snapshot loaded once at batch start; destination-file existence check) -> copy to paths.incomingDirectory staging file -> LibraryCommand.ingestAudioFile commits into DatabaseManager which owns final file placement at sanitized albumID/trackID.ext; staging file removed if ingest throws (MuseAmp/Backend/Library/AudioFileImporter.swift)

Track deletion: deleteTracks(relativePaths:) does read-all-then-filter-then-remove one command per track (non-atomic across the batch); removeAllStoredSongs iterates the same way; MusicLibraryTrackRemovalService logs and continues on per-track failure, so partial removal is possible

Download job records: MusicLibraryDatabase+Downloads is a facade over DatabaseManager (upsert/delete via LibraryCommand, reads via allDownloads with full-scan first(where:) lookups for id/trackID); Backend/Downloads/DownloadStore is the actual owner-consumer per project rules

Artwork repair: resolution order is embedded comment artworkURL -> apiClient.song -> apiClient.album (both at fixed 600x600); downloaded bytes are written atomically to paths.artworkCacheURL(for: trackID) AND embedded into the audio file itself via DownloadArtworkProcessor.embedArtwork, then .artworkDidUpdate posted with AppNotificationUserInfoKey.trackIDs (MuseAmp/Backend/Library/TrackArtworkRepairService.swift)

Background artwork fetch after import runs in Task.detached(priority: .utility) using DownloadArtworkProcessor.cachedArtworkData (Backend/Downloads), posting .artworkDidUpdate per track; failures only warn, leaving tracks without cached artwork

Model adaptation crossing layers: AudioTrackRecord (MuseAmpDatabaseKit) -> PlaylistEntry/CatalogSong/PlaybackTrack/SongExportItem via AudioTrackRecord+AppModels; durationSeconds is converted to rounded milliseconds, zero/negative durations mapped to nil, artworkURL resolved from on-disk cache existence at conversion time, localFileURL derived from paths.absoluteAudioURL(for: relativePath)

Library index rebuild: SongLibraryIndexer sends .rebuildIndex(pruneInvalidFiles: true) to DatabaseManager and trusts its scanned/upserted/deleted counts; an unexpected result variant is logged and reported as all-zero SyncResult rather than thrown
Search: searchTracks loads allTracks() into memory and filters via SearchTextMatcher (Backend/Supplement), capped at 50 results; no DB-side query

Change signaling: all library mutations surface to UI through amusic.* notifications defined in LibraryChangeNotifications.swift with centralized userInfo keys (trackIDs: [String], playlistIDs: [UUID])

result.scope: MuseAmpDatabaseKit local library runtime (WCDB stores, scanner, caches) — MuseAmpDatabaseKit/Sources
result.summary: MuseAmpDatabaseKit is a Swift 6 SwiftPM package whose only dependency is WCDBSwift (Lakr233/wcdb-spm-prebuilt); it owns the file-backed local music library: two SQLite databases, the on-disk audio tree, artwork/lyrics caches, and audit reporting. The central type is `DatabaseManager` (an `@unchecked Sendable` final class) which composes stateless internal structs — `IndexStore` (library_index.sqlite: the derived, rebuildable track index), `StateStore` (library_state.sqlite: authoritative user state — download_jobs, playlists, playlist_entries, meta tables), `LibraryScanner` (disk-to-index rebuild), `LibraryFileManager` (staged .tmp moves into `Audio/<albumID>/<trackID>.<ext>`), `CacheCoordinator` (artwork .jpg / lyrics .lrc caches plus orphan pruning), `DownloadCoordinator` (download-job rows only; actual transport lives in the app target), and `DatabaseBootstrapper` (opens both stores, stamps schema versions in index_meta/state_meta). The public surface is command/event shaped: callers `send(_: LibraryCommand)` (sync fast paths via `sendSynchronouslyIfSupported`, async paths `rebuildIndex`/`ingestAudioFile` isolated to the `@DatabaseActor` global actor) and observe a Combine `events: AnyPublisher<LibraryEvent, Never>` for change notifications (tracksChanged, downloadsChanged, playlistsChanged, cache-changed, audit). `initialize()` must run exactly once (NSLock + precondition); stores are optionals and every public query calls `requireInitialized()` then degrades to empty results if a store is nil. WCDB row types under `Internal/WCDB/` never escape the package — they map to public immutable value models under `Models/` via `init(from:)`/`toModel()`, honoring the repo rule that app code only sees model types. `Storage/LibraryPaths` owns the whole directory layout (Documents/OfflineLibrary base excluded from backup, Caches/OfflineLibrary/LibraryArtwork for purgeable artwork) plus one-time migrations from Application Support and legacy cache directory names. `RuntimeDependencies` injects host-app closures (inspectAudioFile, resolveDownloadURL, fetchLyrics, fetchArtworkData, requestHeaders, setScreenAwake) so the package performs no networking itself; `DatabaseManager+Audit` produces an `AuditSnapshot` of counts, orphans, invalid paths, temp files, and schema mismatches.
conventions: DatabaseManager is split by responsibility into extension files: DatabaseManager+Commands.swift, +Queries.swift, +Writes.swift, +Audit.swift, +Support.swift — mirroring the repo-wide '+Responsibility' split rule; CLAUDE.md explicitly says to keep this split rather than regrow one large file

All internal collaborators (IndexStore, StateStore, LibraryScanner, CacheCoordinator, LibraryFileManager, DownloadCoordinator, DatabaseBootstrapper) are stateless structs holding a WCDB Database/logger reference; only DatabaseManager is a class

WCDB persistence types live in Internal/WCDB/ named XxxRow: Codable + TableCodable with `static let tableName`, snake_case CodingKeys, explicit BindColumnConstraint/BindIndex blocks, and bidirectional `init(from model:)` / `toModel()` mapping to public Models/ structs — rows never cross the package boundary

Public models (AudioTrackRecord, DownloadJob, Playlist, PlaylistEntry, AlbumGroup, AuditSnapshot, ...) are Sendable+Codable+Hashable (mostly Identifiable) structs with fully memberwise public inits, defaulted optionals, and trailing commas in multi-line parameter lists

Logging goes through injectable `LogSink` closure wrapped by DatabaseLogger, always called via free-function shim `DBLog.<level>(logger, "Scope", "key=value message")`; message style is key=value pairs; failures logged at error/critical, successful writes at verbose/info — no print/os_log
Errors are NSError(domain: "DatabaseManager", code: N) with `String(localized:bundle:.module)` messages (codes 0,1,2,4,5 observed); localized strings kept in Resources/Localizable.xcstrings

guard-early-return is pervasive; query paths return empty defaults ([], [:], nil) when the optional store is missing instead of throwing; best-effort cache writes and file deletions use `try?` (some without logging, deviating from the repo's 'log every swallowed error' rule)

Concurrency: a public `@globalActor DatabaseActor` serializes async mutations; `@preconcurrency import` for Combine/WCDBSwift; DatabaseManager is `@unchecked Sendable` with NSLock-guarded one-shot initialization; sync command subset handled off-actor by `sendSynchronouslyIfSupported` returning nil for async-only commands

Path safety: every point where a trackID/albumID/extension becomes a path component routes through the public `sanitizePathComponent` free function (replaces /:\?%*|"<> and control chars, collapses '..', falls back to "unknown"); `String.nilIfEmpty` (trims then nils) is the standard empty-string normalizer
Mutation methods emit a LibraryEvent on eventSubject immediately after the store write, with IDs scoped to what changed (Set<String> trackIDs, Set<UUID> playlist ids)
Meta key/value tables (index_meta, state_meta) store schema/format versions and last-rebuild stamps as strings via metaString/metaInt/setMetaValue private helpers, duplicated per store
dataFlows: Two databases with asymmetric ownership: library_index.sqlite (tracks + index_meta) is DERIVED state, fully rebuildable from disk; library_state.sqlite (download_jobs, playlists, playlist_entries, state_meta) is AUTHORITATIVE user data with no disk fallback. The on-disk audio tree `Documents/OfflineLibrary/Audio/<albumID>/<trackID>.<ext>` is the source of truth for track identity — LibraryScanner derives trackID/albumID purely from path components; embedded metadata (via injected dependencies.inspectAudioFile) only fills display fields.

Ingest flow (DatabaseManager+Writes.ingestAudioFile, @DatabaseActor): source file moved via staging `.tmp` then rename by LibraryFileManager.moveToLibrary — pre-existing destination and stale staging files are deleted first (silent overwrite semantics); file is re-inspected; artwork/lyrics written to caches with `try?` (best-effort, failure does not fail ingest); AudioTrackRecord upserted into IndexStore preserving existing createdAt; the matching DownloadJob row is deleted from StateStore; tracksChanged/downloadsChanged/metadataChanged/artworkCacheChanged/lyricsCacheChanged events emitted.

Rebuild flow (LibraryScanner.rebuildIndexFromDisk, @DatabaseActor): enumerates Audio/, validates relativePath against regex ^[^/]+/[^/]+\.[A-Za-z0-9]+$ and rejects .tmp; skip-unchanged check compares fileSizeBytes equality plus fileModifiedAt within 0.5s of the index snapshot; invalid or uninspectable files are appended to invalidRelativePaths and, when pruneInvalidFiles, deleted from disk (try?); after the walk it upserts changed records in one transaction, deletes index rows for paths not seen or invalid, prunes orphan artwork/lyrics caches against surviving trackIDs, removes empty album directories, then stamps last_rebuild_* and schema versions in index_meta.

Deletion flows (removeTrack/removeAlbumSynchronously): order is disk file first, caches second, index rows third, events last; a thrown disk-removal error aborts before the DB row is deleted (DB never points at a missing file it knowingly deleted, but a partially-failed disk delete leaves the row). removeAlbum deletes the whole album directory then all rows where albumID matches.

Download records: DownloadJobRow primary key is jobID but all queries/deletes/upserts are keyed by trackID; uniqueness per track is enforced only by DownloadCoordinator.enqueue checking `download(trackID:)` before insert — upsertDownload(.insertOrReplace on jobID) from the app side can create a second row for the same trackID. DownloadCoordinator.pauseAll/resumeAll are log-only no-ops; real transfer state machine lives in MuseAmp/Backend/Downloads/DownloadManager which drives .upsertDownloadJob/.deleteDownloadJobs commands.

Playlist mutations rewrite the entire entry set per playlist inside a WCDB transaction (delete-all by playlistID, reinsert with renumbered position, bump playlist.updatedAt). PlaylistEntry rows denormalize track metadata (title/artist/artwork/durationMillis/lyrics) so entries survive track deletion; nothing cascades from track removal to playlists — the audit instead reports unresolvedPlaylistEntryCount against valid index trackIDs.

Caches split across volumes: artwork `<trackID>.jpg` under Caches/OfflineLibrary/LibraryArtwork (OS-purgeable; writeArtwork is write-once — skips if the file exists, refresh only via forceArtwork which clears the whole cache first), lyrics `<trackID>.lrc` under Documents/OfflineLibrary/LibraryLyrics via public LyricsCacheStore. CacheCoordinator prunes both against the index trackID set during rebuild and audit counts orphans.

Boundary crossings: the app layer (MuseAmp/Backend/Library/MusicLibraryDatabase*, PlaylistStore, DownloadManager via AppEnvironment) imports the package, sends LibraryCommands, and subscribes to the events publisher; RuntimeDependencies closures cross back into the app for metadata inspection, URL resolution, lyrics/artwork fetching. LibraryPaths also exposes legacyPlaylistURL (playlists.json) and playbackStateURL consumed by app-side stores.

Versioning/migration: DatabaseFormat pins indexSchemaVersion=1/indexFormatVersion=1/stateSchemaVersion=1; bootstrap only stamps versions (index) or re-runs createTablesIfNeeded + stamp (state) on mismatch — DatabaseResetReason exists but indexResetReason is always nil today, so no destructive reset path is actually wired. LibraryPaths performs one-time moves: Application Support/OfflineLibrary -> Documents/OfflineLibrary at init (old dir deleted if new already exists), and legacy ArtworkCache/LyricsCache directory renames inside ensureDirectoriesExist; the whole base directory is excluded from iCloud backup.

result.scope: LAN sync protocol/sessions + supplements, logging, menu providers (MuseAmp/Backend/Sync, MuseAmp/Backend/Supplement, MuseAmp/Backend/Logging, MuseAmp/Backend/MenuProviders)
result.summary: This scope holds four backend subdomains of a pure-UIKit iOS/tvOS music app. Backend/Sync implements a complete LAN song-transfer stack: SyncProtocol.swift defines all wire/domain models (version-gated protocol v4 via SyncConstants, SyncEndpoint with IPv6/scope-aware host parsing, SyncConnectionInfo for QR handoff, SyncManifest/SyncManifestEntry, SyncPlaylistSession with expected/unique track-count invariants, SyncTransferError); SyncServer.swift is a hand-rolled HTTP/1.1 server over NWListener (routes POST /auth, GET /manifest, GET /track/{id}, password-to-Bearer-token auth via an NSLock-guarded TokenStore, 1 MiB request cap, 64 KiB chunked file streaming, progress callbacks); SyncTransferSession.swift is the @MainActor orchestrator owning both sender state (password, prepared batch, server, Bonjour advertiser) and receiver state (browser, temp download directory), delegating network client calls to APIClient (APIClient+Transfer.swift) and imports to AudioFileImporter. SyncPreparedTrackBuilder(+Export) prepares per-track temp copies with embedded catalog metadata/lyrics/artwork off the main thread; SyncBonjourAdvertiser/Browser handle NetService publish/discover with role+protocolVersion TXT records; TVPlaylistSessionStore persists and strictly validates a JSON playlist-session manifest on tvOS. Most Sync files are mirrored into MuseAmpTV/Backend/Sync via relative symlinks, so they must compile for both targets (tvOS sender path is stubbed to throw). Backend/Logging/AppLog.swift is the single mandated diagnostics facade wrapping Dog with category-name normalization and a Layout sub-channel. Backend/Supplement holds cross-cutting nonisolated utilities: ConcurrencyHelpers (UncheckedSendableBox, OnceGuard, ContinuationOnceGuard, nonAccumulatingTimer, asyncMapLatest), TrackTitleSanitizer (frequency-based trailing-pattern stripping with a lock-guarded static cache refreshed from library titles), ChineseScriptSearchNormalizer/SearchTextMatcher (Hans/Hant search-form expansion and highlight ranges), AVMetadataHelper, and StringUtilities (sanitizedLogText, isCatalogID). Backend/MenuProviders contains injected UIKit menu factories (Song/Playlist/AddToPlaylist/Playback context menus plus stateless Copy/Download/MenuSection/preview helpers) that fetch data at action time through escaping provider closures and mutate state only through PlaylistStore/PlaybackController. State ownership: SyncTransferSession instances are created per-flow by AppEnvironment+Transfer (iOS) and TVAppContext (tvOS); menu providers hold no domain state of their own.
conventions: Pervasive explicit `nonisolated` annotations on types, extensions, and methods (Swift 6 default-isolation style); thread-safe classes are `final nonisolated class X: @unchecked Sendable` with NSLock-guarded `nonisolated(unsafe)` vars (SyncServer.TokenStore, AppLog.configured, TrackTitleSanitizer.trailingPatternCounts, OnceGuard)

Sync* prefix for all transfer types; file split by responsibility: protocol models in SyncProtocol.swift, server in SyncServer.swift, orchestration in SyncTransferSession.swift, file prep split as SyncPreparedTrackBuilder.swift + SyncPreparedTrackBuilder+Export.swift (the +Export extension holds the SongExportItem-dependent pipeline excluded from tvOS)
tvOS compatibility via #if os(tvOS) stubs and shared-symlink compilation (MuseAmpTV/Backend/Sync symlinks); any new Sync file the TV target needs must get a matching symlink

Logging style: AppLog.<level>(self, "methodName key=value key2=value2") with the instance as category kind; user-supplied strings passed through sanitizedLogText(_:maxLength:); errors logged as error=\(error.localizedDescription); entry/exit logging on long operations (prepareBatch, downloadEntries, clearLogs)
Every catch or failure path logs via AppLog before returning/continuing; silent `try?` is rare and confined to cleanup/optional caching (e.g. try? handle.close(), try? lyricsCacheStore?.saveLyrics)

Heavy guard/early-return usage; trailing commas in multi-line argument lists; closures default to non-optional empty defaults for always-set callbacks (onDevicesChanged: ([DiscoveredDevice]) -> Void = { _ in }) but optional for genuinely optional ones (onProgress?)
Continuation safety via shared ContinuationOnceGuard / OnceGuard from ConcurrencyHelpers.swift instead of ad-hoc flags (SyncServer.start)
Ordered de-duplication implemented as a private local Array.orderedUnique() extension duplicated in SyncProtocol.swift and TVPlaylistSessionStore.swift (kept private per-file rather than shared)

Menu providers are final classes injected with stores (PlaylistStore, PlaybackController) and a weak UIViewController for alert presentation; stateless helpers are enums with static factories (CopyMenuProvider, DownloadMenuProvider, MenuSectionProvider)

Menus use UIDeferredMenuElement.uncached and @escaping () -> [T] data providers evaluated at action time; sections grouped via MenuSectionProvider.inline(); async work inside UIAction handlers wrapped in Task { @MainActor in }; toasts only via PlaybackFeedbackPresenter, confirmations via ConfirmationAlertPresenter, input via AlertInputViewController
All user-facing strings via String(localized:); localized errorDescription on LocalizedError enums (SyncEndpointParseError, SyncTransferError)

Background-thread invariants asserted with assert(!Thread.isMainThread) in file-prep paths; detached Task.detached(priority: .userInitiated) with withTaskCancellationHandler for cancellable batch prep
dataFlows: Sender flow: AudioTrackRecord/Playlist (MuseAmpDatabaseKit, read via MusicLibraryDatabase + LibraryPaths.absoluteAudioURL) -> SyncPlaylistTransferPlan filters to on-disk transferable tracks and builds SyncPlaylistSession (orderedTrackIDs preserve duplicates; expected counts derived) -> SyncPreparedTrackBuilder copies/hardlinks each file into a temp dir (am-transfer-<UUID>), conditionally embeds catalog JSON comment + lyrics + artwork via ExportMetadataProcessor/DownloadArtworkProcessor (Backend/Downloads layer), reads duration from AVURLAsset -> PreparedTransferBatch (manifest + filesByTrackID) -> SyncServer serves manifest JSON and streams raw file bytes over plain HTTP on the LAN

Auth/integrity surface: 6-digit numeric password (SyncPasswordGenerator) carried in QR SyncConnectionInfo; SyncServer.TokenStore exchanges it for a UUID Bearer token; protocol compatibility is an exact string match on protocolVersion "4" enforced both in Bonjour discovery (SyncBonjourBrowser drops incompatible devices) and at session level (SyncTransferError.unsupportedProtocolVersion); no TLS, no per-file checksum — file integrity relies on Content-Length and connection-close semantics

Receiver flow: SyncBonjourBrowser/manual endpoint -> SyncTransferSession.authenticate/fetchManifest/downloadTransferTrack all via APIClient (Backend/API/APIClient+Transfer.swift; UI never constructs endpoints) -> files land in temp dir am-transfer-receive-<UUID> named <trackID>.<ext> -> missingEntries() dedupe check compares trackID existence plus |duration delta| > 1.0s against MusicLibraryDatabase -> AudioFileImporter.importFiles(options: .offlineTransfer) ingests into the library (crosses into MuseAmpDatabaseKit); partial cancellation surfaces PartialDownloadError carrying already-downloaded URLs so callers can still import them; temp dir removed in stopReceiver/cleanupReceiverDownloads

tvOS persistence: SyncPlaylistSession (sender-supplied) -> TVPlaylistSessionManifest persisted as pretty-printed JSON via atomic write by TVPlaylistSessionStore (owned by TVAppContext); validate() enforces expectedTrackCount/expectedUniqueTrackCount consistency, exact database track-count match, per-track DB presence, and on-disk audio file existence before declaring the session valid — any mismatch invalidates and asks the user to re-send

Progress/state fan-out: SyncServer reports SyncSenderTransferProgress via @Sendable closure hopping to MainActor in SyncTransferSession.publishSenderProgress; completedTrackIDs Set in SyncServer is mutated from connection callbacks on its serial DispatchQueue (note: connectionIDs/completedTrackIDs are only safe because all NWConnection callbacks share that one queue); SyncBonjourBrowser pushes sorted DiscoveredDevice lists to Interface/Sync via onDevicesChanged

Ownership: SyncTransferSession is constructed per flow by AppEnvironment+Transfer.makeSyncTransferSession() (iOS) and TVAppContext (tvOS); it owns password regeneration (new password + Bonjour token per prepareSender), prepared batch lifecycle (cleanup on stopSender), and receiver temp dir lifecycle

Logging data flow: AppLog.bootstrap(with: LibraryPaths) initializes Dog file logging into LibraryPaths.logsDirectory once (lock-guarded); clearLogs truncates the active file and deletes the rest; all scope code logs through AppLog with sanitizedLogText redaction of device/track names (passwords are never logged, only passwordLength)

TrackTitleSanitizer holds process-global mutable state: refresh(titles:) recomputes trailing-pattern frequency counts from the whole library (called from AppEnvironment events layer) and sanitize() reads them under lock, gated by AppPreferences.isCleanSongTitleEnabled — display-only transformation, never persisted

Menu providers transform UI gestures into store mutations: AddToPlaylistMenuProvider/PlaylistContextMenuProvider write through PlaylistStore (addSong/createPlaylist/rename/duplicate/merge/clear/delete) and PlaybackMenuProvider through PlaybackController (play/playNext/addToQueue/toggleLiked); copy actions write to UIPasteboard; no direct persistence in this folder

Cross-layer touchpoints relevant to integrity: Backend/Sync reads Backend/Models (SongExportItem), Backend/Downloads (ExportMetadataProcessor, DownloadArtworkProcessor), Backend/Lyrics cache (LyricsCacheStore), Backend/API (APIClient), and MuseAmpDatabaseKit (MusicLibraryDatabase, LibraryPaths, AudioFileImporter, Playlist)

result.summary: MuseAmpPlayerKit is a self-contained Swift 6 (strict concurrency, swiftLanguageModes [.v6]) SwiftPM package providing queue-based audio playback with gapless preloading, lock-screen/Control Center integration, and a delegate-based API; its only dependency is Kingfisher (artwork). The central type is `MusicPlayer`, a `@MainActor final class` in Player/MusicPlayer.swift, split across responsibility extensions (+Playback transport and loadAndPlay/continueWithCurrentEngineItem, +Queue public queue editing, +Observation KVO/notification handling and item-failure advance, +Restoration restorePlayback, +Logging describe helpers). Queue truth lives in `PlaybackQueue` (Queue/PlaybackQueue.swift), a value-type struct owning the canonical `items` array, `currentIndex` (an index into the derived `effectiveOrder`, not into `items`), `playedIndices` history, and a `shufflePermutation`; consumers only ever see immutable `QueueSnapshot` values. The AVFoundation layer is abstracted behind the internal `AudioPlaybackEngine` protocol, implemented by `AVPlayerEngine` wrapping an `AVQueuePlayer` plus a tracked `preloadedItem` for gapless transitions (tests inject MockAudioPlaybackEngine through the internal init). MediaCenter/ holds three collaborators owned by MusicPlayer: `MediaCenterCoordinator` (selects `SessionMediaCenterBackend` using MPNowPlayingSession on iOS 16+/tvOS 14+ versus `LegacyMediaCenterBackend` using MPNowPlayingInfoCenter), `NowPlayingInfoManager` (mutates a `currentInfo` dictionary mirror and republishes it wholesale, including a 10-second auto-expiring lyric subtitle and async Kingfisher artwork via `ArtworkLoader`), and `RemoteCommandManager` (wires MPRemoteCommandCenter targets to a weak player reference). `AudioSessionManager` configures AVAudioSession (long-form audio policy with fallback) and routes interruption/route-change notifications back into pause/play. Models/ contains the small public Sendable value types (`PlayerItem`, `PlaybackState`, `RepeatMode`, `TransitionReason`, `QueueSnapshot`), and Logging/ defines the injected `MusicPlayerLogger` protocol with a `NoopMusicPlayerLogger` default. Nothing is persisted inside the package; session persistence and URL resolution live in the app's `MuseAmp/Backend/Playback/` (PlaybackController, PlaybackSessionStore), which is the package's primary consumer along with the MuseAmpTV shell.
conventions: Responsibility-based extension file splits: MusicPlayer.swift core + MusicPlayer+Playback/+Queue/+Observation/+Restoration/+Logging.swift; same pattern for PlaybackQueue+Shuffle.swift
All stateful service classes are @MainActor final class; PlaybackQueue is a struct with mutating methods; public models are Sendable value types (per repo prefer-value-types rule)

Dependency injection via init parameters with defaulted NoopMusicPlayerLogger(); no singletons; protocol seams for testability (AudioPlaybackEngine, MediaCenterBackend, NowPlayingStatePublishing, RemoteCommandCenterProviding) with a second internal init taking the seam (MusicPlayer(engine:logger:), MediaCenterCoordinator(backend:backendKind:))
Visibility split: only MusicPlayer, its delegate protocol, the Models types, and MusicPlayerLogger are public; engine, queue, and all managers are internal

Logging style: each class defines a private log(_:_:) helper hard-coding a component string; MusicPlayer adds describe(item:)/describe(queue:) formatters; .info on entry of every public method, .verbose for completion/no-op detail, .warning for ignored calls using the phrasing 'X ignored because Y'; the message parameter is @autoclosure
Guard-based early returns everywhere, almost always paired with a log line inside the guard else-block

Async callback hops: KVO blocks, notification handlers, and time observers immediately wrap work in Task { @MainActor [weak self] in } and store observers in dedicated properties (NSKeyValueObservation, NSObjectProtocol, Any timeObserver) torn down via explicit teardownItemObservers()/teardownObservers()
Platform gating via #if os(iOS) || os(tvOS) (plus watchOS for AVAudioSession); ArtworkLoader has a full no-op fallback class for other platforms
Delegate protocol ships default no-op implementations in a public extension so conformers implement only what they need
Trailing commas in multi-line argument lists and parameter lists (formatter-enforced style)
Localized user-facing strings use String(localized:bundle:.module) with keys mirrored in Resources/Localizable.xcstrings
Uniform file headers 'Created by @Lakr233 on 2026/04/11.'; MARK sections inside larger files (Authoritative State / Derived / Navigation / Queue Editing / Modes / Private Helpers)
Doc comments (///) are reserved for index-semantics contracts (e.g. 'Index into effectiveOrder', upcoming-view index conventions) and protocol behavior guarantees
dataFlows: Queue state ownership: PlaybackQueue holds four mutually-dependent structures — canonical items[], currentIndex (index into effectiveOrder, NOT items), playedIndices (canonical indices in play order), shufflePermutation. Every insert/remove/move performs manual index bookkeeping (compactMap shift-down in removeCanonicalIndex, adjustPlayedIndicesAfterInsert, rebuildPlayedIndicesAfterReorder, clearUpcoming rebuilds an old-to-new index map). This is the single hottest data-integrity surface in the scope; public queue-editing indices are interpreted relative to the 'upcoming' view (ci + 1 + upcomingIndex)

No persistence inside the package. Restoration data flows in from the app layer (MuseAmp/Backend/Playback/PlaybackSessionStore.swift) via MusicPlayer.restorePlayback -> PlaybackQueue.restore, which synthesizes playedIndices = 0..<currentIndex and, when shuffled=true, an identity permutation Array(0..<items.count) — the original shuffle order is not round-tripped, so a restored shuffled queue plays canonical order until reshuffled

State fan-out: every transition (loadAndPlay, continueWithCurrentEngineItem, restorePlayback) repeats the same sync block to three sinks — delegate callbacks (consumed by the app's PlaybackController and TV shell), NowPlayingInfoManager (system now-playing info), RemoteCommandManager.updateEnabledCommands/updateLikeCommand. The three near-duplicate blocks in MusicPlayer+Playback.swift and +Restoration.swift can drift; reviewers should diff them

Gapless preload invariant: MusicPlayer.preloadNextItem hands an AVPlayerItem for upcoming.first to AVPlayerEngine, which tracks it by identity (preloadedItem) inside AVQueuePlayer. Queue mutations must re-call preloadNextItem to keep engine state consistent with PlaybackQueue; insertInQueue/removeFromQueue(at:)/moveInQueue only refresh when index==0 (or source/destination==0), while playNext/removeFromQueue(id:)/shuffled/repeatMode setters always refresh. advance paths (next(), handleItemEnd) consume the preload via advanceToPreloadedItem() -> continueWithCurrentEngineItem, else rebuild via loadAndPlay

Failure flow: AVPlayerItem .status KVO (MusicPlayer+Observation.swift) -> didFailItem delegate -> playbackQueue.advance() -> loadAndPlay(reason: .itemFailed); continueWithCurrentEngineItem separately re-checks avItem.status == .failed because a preloaded item can fail before observation attaches. Both synthesize an NSError(domain: "MuseAmpPlayerKit", code: -1) with a localized fallback message

Now-playing info flow: NowPlayingInfoManager keeps currentInfo: [String: Any] as the source dictionary and assigns it wholesale to the backend on every change; staleness guards compare currentItemID before applying async artwork (ArtworkLoader via KingfisherManager.shared, sharing the app's Kingfisher cache) and before restoring the artist after the 10s lyric-subtitle expiration Task

Media center backend selection crosses OS-version boundaries: MPNowPlayingSession (session backend, automaticallyPublishesNowPlayingInfo=false, becomeActiveIfPossible on play) vs MPNowPlayingInfoCenter.default()+MPRemoteCommandCenter.shared() (legacy backend); RemoteCommandManager registers/unregisters all command targets on startPlayback/stop and holds the player weakly

Dead code relevant to an integrity audit: PlaybackQueue.rebuildShufflePermutationIndices(), the three helpers in PlaybackQueue+Shuffle.swift besides generateShufflePermutation (insertIntoPermutation, removeFromPermutation, adjustPermutationAfterCanonicalRemoval), and AudioPlaybackEngine.hasAdvancedToPreloadedItem() have zero call sites in Sources or Tests — removeCanonicalIndex reimplements permutation adjustment inline instead of using them

Lifecycle note: MusicPlayer has no deinit cleanup by design ('Cleanup is handled by stop()'); stop() tears down observers, clears the queue and now-playing info, unregisters remote commands, and deactivates the audio session, but AudioSessionManager interruption/route observers intentionally live for the player's lifetime (teardown() exists but is never called from MusicPlayer)

result.scope: App lifecycle, environment, root shell, extensions (MuseAmp/Application, MuseAmp/Interface/Root, MuseAmp/Extension, MuseAmp/main.swift)
result.summary: This scope is the iOS app's bootstrap chain and adaptive root shell. Entry is manual: main.swift wraps UIApplicationMain in MainActor.assumeIsolated -> AppDelegate (minimal: AlertController theming, scene config, Mac Catalyst Cmd+W/Q interception with a quit-confirmation gate driven by the pure-function MacCatalystTerminationPolicy, background-URLSession completion-handler storage, and a global terminateApplication() -> Never) -> SceneDelegate, which shows BootProgressController as the initial root, builds AppEnvironment off-main (DatabaseManager.initializeSynchronously inside a Task), then cross-fades to MainController and triggers playback restoration plus downloadManager.reconcileOnLaunch(). AppEnvironment is the composition root and sole owner of all backend services (~18 stored lets: APIClient, DatabaseManager, MusicLibraryDatabase, PlaylistStore, DownloadStore, DownloadManager, PlaybackController, lyrics services, AudioFileImporter, etc.), with responsibility-split extensions: +Bootstrap (static DatabaseManager factory injecting RuntimeDependencies closures across the app->MuseAmpDatabaseKit boundary, AppLog sink bridging, Kingfisher cache config), +Events (Combine subscription to databaseManager.events that fans out as amusic.* NotificationCenter posts and refreshes the global TrackTitleSanitizer), and +Transfer (SyncTransferSession factory). MainController is the adaptive root: it computes LayoutMode (.compact/.relaxed/.catalyst) from trait/layout passes and swaps between a child TabBarController (compact: UITab on iOS 18+, legacy viewControllers on 16-17, conditional Search tab keyed off AppPreferences.currentSubsonicConfiguration) and a PopupBarSplitViewController hosting SidebarViewController plus cached per-destination/per-playlist UINavigationControllers. The LNPopupController "Now Playing" bar is managed twice in deliberately duplicated code (TabBarController+Popup and MainController+Popup) binding PlaybackController $snapshot/playbackTimeSubject, with PopupBarPagingHandler translating popup-bar swipes into skipToQueueTrack via LNPopupItem.userInfo queue indexes under a 0.5s cooldown. SceneDelegate also owns all external data entry: file-URL imports (audio extension whitelist, .museampplaylist, .subsonicconfig) coalesced over 200ms and queued until MainController exists, plus museamp://tv URLs carrying base64-JSON SyncReceiverHandshakeInfo with protocol-version gating. Extension/ holds five tiny Extension+ClassName.swift files (Bundle.appIcon, String.sanitizedTrackTitle delegating to TrackTitleSanitizer, UIColor.shiftedHue, UITableView header/footer autosizing, UIView.removeAnimationsRecursively). AppPreferences is a nonisolated static enum facade over ConfigurableKit/UserDefaults holding Subsonic server URL normalization (forces https + /rest suffix), credentials, and sort-option persistence.
conventions: Responsibility-split files with + suffixes: AppEnvironment+Bootstrap/+Events/+Transfer, MainController+Layout/+Popup/+Sidebar, TabBarController+Popup/+Reselection; system-type extensions named Extension+ClassName.swift
Manual UIApplicationMain in main.swift wrapped in MainActor.assumeIsolated; no @main, no Main.storyboard
Dependency injection of AppEnvironment through initializers everywhere; child controllers and menu providers declared as lazy properties initialized with environment; no singletons introduced

All diagnostics through AppLog (including scoped AppLog.Layout.info); error/warning logged before every silent fallback; DatabaseKit log levels bridged into AppLog via the logSink closure in +Bootstrap

Combine style: single `var cancellables: Set<AnyCancellable> = []` per class, custom `.removeDuplicates { }` closures per concern (track/state/upcoming vs time/duration), `.receive(on: DispatchQueue.main)` before `.sink`, high-frequency progress via playbackTimeSubject not $snapshot
Heavy guard/early-return usage including `guard let self else { return }` in closures; `required init?(coder:)` marked @available(*, unavailable) with fatalError
Non-optional callback closures with empty defaults (onBootComplete, onDestinationSelected, onPlaylistSelected); one deviation: SidebarViewController.onImportPlaylistRequested is `(() -> Void)?`
Cooldown via `var cooldownDate: Date = .distantPast` checked with `Date() < cooldownDate` (PopupBarPagingHandler), per the property rules

`nonisolated` enums for shared value types (RootDestination, LayoutMode, SidebarSection, SidebarItem); `nonisolated(unsafe)` NotificationCenter observer tokens paired with MainActor.assumeIsolated inside the observer block (SidebarViewController)
Platform/availability splits with #if targetEnvironment(macCatalyst) and if #available(iOS 18.0/26.0) including divergent tab ordering (settings before search pre-26, search appended on 26)
Then `.then { }` for inline config, SnapKit `make.edges.equalToSuperview()` for layout, Interface.transition/springAnimate/quickAnimate wrappers instead of raw UIView.animate
Accessibility identifiers namespaced by feature: popup.playPause, popup.next, nav.albums, tab.search, main.tabbar
Localized user-facing strings via String(localized:); notification names namespaced amusic.* with userInfo keys through AppNotificationUserInfoKey
Trailing commas in multi-line argument lists throughout this scope

Deliberate code duplication between TabBarController+Popup and MainController+Popup (popup item updates, artwork loading, context menu construction) rather than a shared abstraction; both delegate paging to the shared PopupBarPagingHandler
Diffable data sources only (SidebarViewController uses UICollectionViewDiffableDataSource with reconfigureItems for in-place playlist updates; initial snapshot applied non-animated in viewDidLoad)
dataFlows: Ownership chain: BootProgressController creates DatabaseManager + AppEnvironment in a background Task (fatalError if the convenience-init path fails); SceneDelegate retains the environment and a weak MainController; AppEnvironment then owns every backend service for the app lifetime and threads them by injection

App->MuseAmpDatabaseKit boundary crossing via RuntimeDependencies closures (AppEnvironment+Bootstrap): resolveDownloadURL goes through apiClient.playback(id:); fetchArtworkData uses raw URLSession.shared.data (bypasses APIClient — integrity-audit-relevant exception); inspectAudioFile derives identity from on-disk layout (albumID = first relative-path component, trackID = filename sans extension), making the audio directory structure a data contract

Playback state persistence: SceneDelegate calls playbackController.persistPlaybackState() on willResignActive, didEnterBackground, didDisconnect, and Catalyst prepareForImmediateTermination; restorePersistedPlaybackIfNeeded() + downloadManager.reconcileOnLaunch() run once after boot — a crash loses only state since the last lifecycle hook

External data ingress is concentrated in SceneDelegate: file URLs classified by extension (10-entry audio whitelist, PlaylistTransferFileType extension, .subsonicconfig) and museamp://tv URLs decoded from base64 JSON into SyncReceiverHandshakeInfo with SyncConstants protocol-version compatibility check; imports are coalesced 200ms (Files app delivers one openURLContexts per file) and queued until MainController exists; only the FIRST server profile in a batch is imported, the rest dropped with a warning; the Documents/Inbox directory is wiped after audio import (MainController.cleanupInbox)

Database events fan-out: databaseManager.events (Combine) -> AppEnvironment+Events.handleDatabaseEvent -> NotificationCenter posts (.libraryDidSync on indexRebuildFinished/tracksChanged; .artworkDidUpdate carrying trackIDs userInfo) consumed by feature UI; the same path refreshes TrackTitleSanitizer global state from all track titles, which String.sanitizedTrackTitle (Extension+String) reads everywhere popup titles are rendered. Note: the .sink in observeDatabaseEvents captures self strongly while stored in self.cancellables — a retain cycle, tolerated because AppEnvironment is app-lifetime

Subsonic credentials (server URL, username, password) are stored via ConfigurableKit in UserDefaults-backed storage (AppPreferences) — password is plaintext in defaults; set/clear posts .serverConfigurationDidChange which structurally mutates the UI (Search tab added/removed in TabBarController, search/downloads rows rebuilt in SidebarViewController with fallback-to-albums when the selected destination disappears); sort options are written directly to UserDefaults.standard via the generic storedSortOption helpers

Popup bar derives entirely from PlaybackController: $snapshot (deduped two ways), playbackTimeSubject for high-frequency progress, and ConfigurableKit clean-title publisher; writes back only through playbackController.togglePlayPause/next/skipToQueueTrack(at:); LNPopupItem.userInfo carries untyped ['trackID': String, 'queueIndex': Int] — queueIndex can go stale against queue mutations, mitigated by PopupBarPagingHandler's 0.5s cooldown plus deferred re-sync

Popup artwork loading guards against staleness three ways before assignment: task not cancelled, popupArtworkURL still matches, AND playbackController.snapshot.currentTrack?.artworkURL still matches; local file URLs are read synchronously via UIImage(contentsOfFile:), remote via KingfisherManager (duplicated in both popup extensions and PopupBarPagingHandler)

Sidebar playlist covers resolve local-first: PlaylistStore.coverImageData wins, else PlaylistCoverArtworkCache composes from per-entry artwork where paths.artworkCacheURL(for: trackID) on disk is preferred over APIClient.resolveMediaURL(entry.artworkURL, ...) — stale results discarded via representedPlaylistID check; sidebar shows liked-songs playlist pinned plus 7 most-recently-updated others

MainController caches UINavigationControllers per RootDestination and per playlist UUID; stale playlist controllers are pruned against the active-ID set reported by sidebar reloads, with selection fallback to .albums when the selected playlist was deleted; LayoutMode transitions tear down and reinstall child hierarchies and rebind/unbind the popup Combine subscriptions (cancellables.removeAll on unbind)

Mac Catalyst termination decisions cross from AppDelegate into the active SceneDelegate's AppEnvironment (playbackController.snapshot + downloadManager.hasExecutingTasks) through MacCatalystTerminationPolicy; last-window-close exits after a 200ms grace check of remaining window scenes, persisting playback state first

conventions: File splits: one type per file under Models/; raw wire DTOs live in Models/Subsonic/ with a Subsonic* prefix and per-endpoint *Payload wrapper structs (SubsonicSongPayload, SubsonicAlbumPayload, SubsonicSearchResultPayload, SubsonicLyricsPayload); app-facing DTOs use a Catalog* prefix mimicking Apple Music API shapes, split into Catalog{Song,Album,Artist}.swift + *Attributes.swift + *Relationships.swift files; top-level service envelopes are named *Response (SearchResponse, SongResponse, AlbumResponse, LyricsResponse).

All mutable shared state is wrapped: ResponseCache and RequestCoalescer are actors; the single mutable var on the Sendable final class SubsonicMusicService (authMode) is `nonisolated(unsafe)` guarded by an NSLock with currentAuthMode()/updateAuthMode() accessors.

Pure mapping functions (mapSong, mapAlbum, mapArtist, mapSearchResponse, map*Placeholder) are static and grouped in a `private extension SubsonicMusicService` at the bottom of SubsonicMusicService.swift — mapping is never done inline in public methods.

Defensive decoding: every Subsonic DTO implements custom init(from:) using shared KeyedDecodingContainer lossy helpers (decodeLossyString, decodeLossyStringIfPresent, decodeLossyIntIfPresent, decodeLossyInt64IfPresent, defined at the bottom of SubsonicMusicService.swift) to tolerate servers that return ints where strings are expected and vice versa; arrays default via `decodeIfPresent(...) ?? []`.

Guard style: `guard x.isEmpty == false else` / `guard ... == false` is preferred over `!x`; early returns throughout; Swift 5.9+ switch expressions for computed properties (cacheIdentifier, actionName, errorDescription).
Swift 6.2 tooling: trailing commas in multi-line argument lists, everything Sendable, file header comments with author @Lakr233 and creation date.

No logging at all inside this package (no AppLog/Dog/print) — diagnostics are expected to happen in the app-side wrapper (APIClient logs per the repo Logging Rules); errors instead carry rich localized messages via String(localized:bundle:.module) including decode-context detail (missing key / type mismatch / coding path).

Public model structs expose full public memberwise inits with `= nil` defaults for optional fields so the app layer can construct them; identity types (CatalogSong, CatalogAlbum, CatalogArtist) override == and hash(into:) to compare id (+type) only, ignoring attributes.

Endpoint construction is centralized in the SubsonicEndpoint enum (actionName / fixedQueryItems / requestQueryItems split into a private extension); each endpoint also defines a `cacheIdentifier` string used to build cache and coalescing keys.
Generic request pipeline: every public API method funnels through the single private `perform(_:cache:decode:)` generic, passing a typed ResponseCache and an @escaping @Sendable decode/map closure.
dataFlows: Request flow: public API (ping/search/album/song/playback/lyrics) -> perform() -> SubsonicEndpoint.url(baseURL:authorization:) builds the URL with auth query items -> URLSession.data -> decodeSubsonicResponse gates on SubsonicResponse envelope status=="ok" (else throws APIError.subsonicRequestFailed) -> Subsonic* DTO -> static mappers produce Catalog*/Response models -> returned to the app's APIClient (MuseAmp/Backend/API/APIClient.swift), the only consumer that constructs SubsonicMusicService (it is rebuilt on credential change; see lines 41 and 222).

Caching (in-memory): five per-response-type ResponseCache actors (search/song/album/playback/lyrics) keyed by "baseURL|username|endpoint.cacheIdentifier"; cacheTTL is hardcoded `.infinity`, so any in-memory hit is permanently fresh for the process lifetime — invalidation only happens via new service instances or cacheVersion bumps. staleValue() has a separate 7-day maxAge used only by the failure-fallback path.

Caching (disk): raw pre-decode response Data is persisted through the injected CacheStorageProvider protocol as a versioned CacheEnvelope (data, cachedAt, version; current cacheVersion=2 — mismatched versions are deleted on read). NOTE: the app currently constructs SubsonicMusicService WITHOUT a cacheStorageProvider (nil default), so the disk path is exercised only by package tests (Tests/SubsonicClientKitTests/CacheTests.swift); the in-memory caches are the live ones.

Stale fallback: on APIError.transportFailed or 5xx (isFallbackEligible) for cacheable requests, perform() serves a stale in-memory value (≤7 days) or re-decodes stale disk data before rethrowing the original error — review for serving outdated catalog data silently; CancellationError is always rethrown immediately.
