Confirmed: line 440 of AlbumDetailViewController.swift is `let count = 64`, a bare magic literal with a meaningless name feeding skeleton row generation in the loading branch of applySnapshot(). AGENTS.md does not endorse inline magic numbers (its only hardcode rule is the unrelated 200 pt lyrics/queue spacer), and the same file already declares `private static let` configuration (releaseDateParser/releaseDateDisplay), so hoisting to `private static let skeletonRowCount = 64` matches existing local style. The adjacent `.map { AlbumItem.skeleton(index: $0) }` makes intent partially inferable, so this is cosmetic — severity low.

Confirmed: lines 381, 389, 422 of AlbumDetailViewController.swift set cell.selectionStyle = .none on AlbumHeaderCell, AlbumTrackSkeletonCell, and DetailFooterCell, all of which inherit TableBaseCell (MuseAmp/Interface/Common/TableBaseCell.swift) whose init already sets selectionStyle = .none and installs a hidden selectedBackgroundView. The only path that could make the reassignment load-bearing — TableBaseCell.setEditing flipping selectionStyle to .default — never fires because AlbumDetailViewController never uses editing mode. AGENTS.md Cell Rules explicitly say cells inherit TableBaseCell for this and 'Never use selectionStyle to remove the gray tap highlight', so the flagged pattern violates rather than follows convention; the .track case in the same closure already omits the assignment, so deletion improves internal consistency. Cosmetic-only, hence low severity.

Verified: line 285 of AlbumDetailViewController.swift calls track.attributes.artwork?.imageURL(width:600,height:600), the only such direct call in the MuseAmp/MuseAmpTV app targets; line 376 of the same file uses environment.apiClient.mediaURL(from:width:height:). AGENTS.md Artwork URL Rules (lines 257-261) explicitly require resolving via apiClient.mediaURL and forbid constructing artwork URLs without going through APIClient.resolveMediaURL, so the flagged pattern violates the documented convention. The divergence is also behavioral: Artwork.imageURL only substitutes {w}/{h} and does URL(string:), while APIClient.mediaURL additionally handles protocol-relative "//" URLs and resolves scheme-less URLs against baseURL — so exported song artwork can break for URL forms the rest of the app handles. environment.apiClient is already in scope in this controller, so the suggested fix matches surrounding style exactly.

Duplication confirmed: `currentTasks.contains { $0.state != .failed }` at lines 166 and 316, and `currentTasks.contains { $0.state == .waitingForNetwork }` at lines 187 and 318, with the BarMenuState equality short-circuit at line 143. The finding slightly overstates the consequence — the bar menu uses UIDeferredMenuElement.uncached, so menu contents are rebuilt at presentation and predicate drift would only misfire the bar-item refresh gate, not show stale actions. But the seam is real: BarMenuState fields silently mirror inline predicates in buildMenuElements, and the fix (private computed properties used at both sites) matches AGENTS.md's explicit rule that values derivable from other state should be computed properties. Nothing in AGENTS.md or the surrounding module endorses duplicating these predicates. Low severity: maintenance-only hazard with no current user-visible effect.

Confirmed: AlbumNavigationHelper.swift lines 76-80 wrap databaseManager.tracks(inAlbumID:) in do/catch that returns nil without any AppLog call. This directly violates the project's explicit Logging Rule ("Every catch block ... that silently swallows an error must log via AppLog.error or AppLog.warning"). The dominant local convention confirms the fix style: all five other tracks(inAlbumID:) catch blocks in the app (SongLibraryViewController+Table.swift:136/203, +Actions.swift:144/187/204, PlaylistDetailViewController+Actions.swift:256) log AppLog.error with albumID and error before returning a fallback. The suggested one-line log matches surrounding repo style exactly and would not make the code read unlike the rest of the module. A DB failure here silently downgrades navigation to a stub album with no diagnostic trail, which actively misleads anyone debugging empty/wrong album-detail reports, so medium severity stands.

Verified: refreshVisibleCells() (DownloadsViewController.swift:324-335) indexes currentTasks[indexPath.row] while the diffable source is keyed by trackID and tasksByTrackID exists precisely for ID-based resolution (used by the cell provider at line 247). Correctness depends on the subtle invariant that currentTasks order matches the applied snapshot, made more delicate by the isShowingRowContextMenu deferral path where currentTasks is replaced while the displayed snapshot is stale. AGENTS.md does not endorse index-based resolution, and the dominant repo convention in sibling browse/playlist controllers is dataSource.itemIdentifier(for:) — so the suggested fix aligns with, rather than violates, local style. Current call sites preserve the order invariant, so no live bug; this is a low-severity clarity/fragility finding.

Verified: SongsViewController+Table.swift:100-126 and AlbumDetailViewController+Table.swift:75-106 contain line-for-line equivalent implementations of the AGENTS "Playback Interaction Rules" song-tap policy (playing/buffering check, seek(to:0) rewind for current track, playNext + switch on .alreadyQueued -> next() / .queued -> PlaybackFeedbackPresenter toast). Grep confirms these are the only two UI sites switching on alreadyQueued and the only UI sites checking latestSnapshot.state == .playing || .buffering. AGENTS.md documents the tap policy but does not endorse per-controller inline copies; the repo's dominant convention is centralization (PlaybackController owns state, PlaybackMenuProvider centralizes menu playback actions with injected track/queue/source closures, PlaybackFeedbackPresenter centralizes toasts), so the suggested canonical entry point matches local style. A third tap site (PlaylistDetailViewController.playSong(at:)) already behaves differently, showing real drift risk. Medium severity: duplicated behavioral policy that a future rule change must edit in both places, with silent UX inconsistency if one is missed; not yet a demonstrated bug.

Confirmed: AnimatedTextLabel.swift lines 53-58 call UIView.transition directly with a hand-built options set that includes .beginFromCurrentState but omits .allowUserInteraction; AGENTS.md line 133 explicitly forbids direct UIView.transition calls and mandates the Interface wrappers, and Interface.transition (Interface/Common/Style/Interface.swift:91-105) would supply both interruptible options automatically. Line 8's layer.removeAllAnimations() in the disablesAnimations didSet violates AGENTS.md line 136. Grep shows this is the only direct UIView.transition/animate call in the app target outside the wrapper file itself, so it is an outlier, not a local convention. The suggested fix (Interface.transition with animations: {}) compiles against the wrapper's signature and aligns the code with the rest of the repo. AGENTS.md explicitly forbids — not endorses — the flagged pattern, so conventionEndorsed=false.

Confirmed: lines 22 and 41 of ImageQuickLookPreviewPresenter.swift use `try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at:)` with no logging, while the same class logs its write failures via AppLog (lines 69, 77). AGENTS.md line 203 explicitly requires every error-swallowing `try?` to log via AppLog.error/AppLog.warning, and the dominant repo convention for removeItem is do/catch + AppLog (DownloadArtworkProcessor, ExportMetadataProcessor, DownloadManager+Persistence, PlaybackSessionStore, Sync* stores all do this). The suggested fix matches local style rather than fighting it. Severity lowered to low: the swallowed error only concerns cleanup of OS-reclaimed temp files, so it cannot corrupt state or breed bugs — it is a diagnostics/convention gap, not actively misleading code.

Verified: ImageQuickLookPreviewPresenter.makePreviewFileURL (lines 52-80) and MuseAmpImageView+Preview.makePreviewFileURL (lines 39-69) duplicate the encode-and-write core verbatim — png-vs-jpeg(0.98) choice, temporaryDirectory+UUID path, atomic write, and identical AppLog warning/error strings. Both files also carry separate QLPreviewControllerDataSource implementations with the same `previewItemURL! as NSURL` force unwrap (line 89 / line 95). Surrounding logic differs (LRU caching keyed by sourceURL and extension-from-URL in the image view; filename sanitization and deinit cleanup in the presenter), so the suggestion's secondary idea of sharing the dataSource is weak, but extracting the temp-file writer is sound, both call sites are already in Interface/Common, and AGENTS.md explicitly places cross-feature shared UI infrastructure there — no convention endorses the duplication. A change to encoding/quality/temp strategy in one copy will silently miss the other, so medium severity is justified.

Confirmed in MuseAmp/Interface/Collections/AlbumHeaderView.swift: applyButtonStyle(to:filled:) (167-210) has two branches identical except for .filled()/.gray() and two color lines, and makeActionButton(systemImage:) (212-232) repeats the same ~10-line configuration block a third time verbatim. Grep shows this pattern exists nowhere else in the repo, so it is not a deliberate convention, and AGENTS.md says nothing endorsing it. The duplication is more than cosmetic: makeActionButton sets the initial button appearance while applyButtonStyle rebuilds it on size-class changes, so an edit to one copy but not the others produces buttons that visibly change style after the first trait change. The suggested single configuration factory fits existing local style (the file already uses private make* helpers) and violates no AGENTS.md rule. Severity stays medium because the cross-function sync requirement genuinely breeds bugs, though it is contained to one file.

Confirmed: `hasAccessory` (line 16) is written once exactly where `accessoryButton` is added to contentView (lines 35-37) and read only to guard that add; it is a stored duplicate of `accessoryButton.superview != nil`. AGENTS.md Property Rules explicitly forbid stored properties tracking state available from an existing source of truth and require derived state to be computed. The repo's other `hasX` flags (hasPreparedInitialData, hasLoadedArtwork, the mandated hasAppliedInitialSnapshot) track facts not derivable from other state, so no local convention endorses this duplication. The suggested fix (superview check, or better, pre-adding in init and toggling isHidden) aligns with the AGENTS.md pre-alloc rule and is a safe drop-in — prepareForReuse already forces the lazy button's creation, so the lazy-trigger objection doesn't hold. Severity remains low: the button is never removed, so the flag cannot currently desync; this is purely a clarity/state-modeling improvement.

Facts verified in /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Interface/Browse/Songs/SongsViewController+Table.swift: lines 14-95 are UITableViewDelegate, lines 99-209 are a "Playback & Navigation" extension (playTrack, openAlbumForTrack, confirmDeleteTrack/deleteTrack, buildContextMenu, makeRepairArtworkAction), and lines 213-255 are UISearchResultsUpdating plus the full performSearch debounce implementation. The finding's description is accurate.

However, only part of it survives adversarial comparison with the surrounding module's dominant convention:
- The playback/menu-helper portion is largely conventional, not a defect: sibling +Table files do the same — SongLibraryViewController+Table.swift has an "Album Navigation & Helpers" extension with buildMenu/openAlbum/openAlbumForTrack/playbackTracks (lines 111-220), and PlaylistViewController+Table.swift has "Navigation & Preview Helpers" and "Subtitle Helpers" extensions. Moving buildContextMenu/playTrack out of +Table (as the suggestion proposes) would make Songs read UNLIKE its Browse siblings, so that half of the suggestion is refuted under the repository-conventions principle.
- The search portion is a genuine deviation: every other searchable controller in the repo keeps UISearchResultsUpdating + performSearch in a dedicated +Search.swift (Interface/Browse/Albums/SongLibraryViewController+Search.swift, Interface/Playlist/PlaylistViewController+Search.swift, Interface/Search/SearchViewController+Search.swift). SongsViewController is the lone outlier embedding search in +Table, and AGENTS.md UIKit File Rules explicitly names "+Search.swift" as a responsibility file. A SongsViewController+Search.swift mirroring SongLibraryViewController+Search.swift would be a strict consistency improvement with no convention conflict.

AGENTS.md does not endorse the grab-bag pattern itself (it mandates responsibility-based splits), so conventionEndorsed=false. Because half the complaint targets the module's deliberate convention and the remaining real issue (misplaced search code, ~43 lines, clearly MARKed) is a cosmetic organization/consistency matter rather than something that misleads readers into bugs, severity is adjusted down to low.

Confirmed at lines 28-30, 36-38, 64-66 of ProgressActionPresenter.swift: each dismiss completion has `guard let viewController else { return }`, invokes the callback, then `_ = viewController` purely to suppress the unused-binding warning. Repo-wide grep shows this `_ =` suppressor pattern exists only in this file; all other `guard let viewController` sites genuinely use the binding, so the dominant local convention is to bind only when used. AGENTS.md says nothing endorsing the pattern. The fix (`guard viewController != nil`) is the compiler's own suggested idiom, keeps the alive-check intent, and has no semantic downside since callbacks capture and guard their own weak viewController. Cosmetic-only impact, so severity remains low.

Verified: SongLibraryViewController.swift lines 357-368 pass .systemFont(ofSize: 16)/.systemFont(ofSize: 13) to SearchHighlightHelper so attributed text matches AmSongCell's private titleLabel/subtitleLabel fonts (AmSongCell.swift lines 25/32, exactly 16 and 13). The same literals are re-typed in PlaylistSearchCell.swift (lines 37, 47, 52) and SearchViewController.swift (lines 183, 192, 216-224) — 7+ call sites across 3 files coupled to private label fonts with no named link between them. AGENTS.md does not endorse the pattern; the repo already uses static style namespaces (InterfaceStyle.Insets/Spacing, AmSongCell's private Layout enum), so the suggested static titleFont/subtitleFont constants fit existing conventions rather than violating them. Inline fonts inside a cell's own label setup are normal repo style, but cross-file duplication that must silently stay in sync with another type's private properties is not an endorsed convention. Severity medium: the invariant is invisible per-file, and a future cell font change would silently mismatch highlighted vs plain rows.

Verified: presentLyricSelectionSheet(with:activeIndex:) in NowPlayingLyricShellController.swift has zero call sites repo-wide; the two +LyricSheet conformance files are empty extensions that wire nothing. Confirmed: SongLibraryViewController.swift:284/302 and SongsViewController.swift:275 force-cast dequeued cells (`as!`), while AlbumDetailViewController (371-374, 394-399) and DownloadsViewController (246-254) in the same Interface/Browse scope use `guard let ... as? ... else { return UITableViewCell() }` for the identical operation. The two idioms also coexist repo-wide (force-cast in Search/PlaylistViewController; guarded in PlaylistDetail/NowPlayingQueue/Sync), so neither is a dominant deliberate convention and standardizing would not make the code read unlike the repo. AGENTS.md has no dequeue rule; its SwifterSwift highlight of dequeueReusableCell(withClass:for:) is compatible with the suggestion. The inconsistency is genuine but purely mechanical/cosmetic — both idioms are standard UIKit and the casts cannot realistically fail given same-file registrations — so severity stays low.

Verified against both files. The duplication and drift are real: /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Interface/Browse/Songs/SongsViewController+Table.swift (performSearch, lines 230-254) and /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Interface/Browse/Albums/SongLibraryViewController+Search.swift (performSearch, lines 31-63 — note the file is under Browse/Albums/, not Browse/Songs/ as the finding implied) both implement the identical 300ms-debounced `db.searchTracks(query:)` flow with divergent mechanics exactly as described: (1) staleness guard via `Task.isCancelled` only vs `currentQuery == query` recheck; (2) `try? await Task.sleep` + separate isCancelled guard vs `do/catch { return }`; (3) error handling that actually diverges behaviorally — Songs aborts and keeps prior results on a DB error, SongLibrary swallows the error inside the detached closure and applies an EMPTY search snapshot — plus `AppLog.error(self, ...)` vs a hardcoded string tag. A maintainer cannot tell which of these differences is intentional, and the divergent error behavior is the kind of drift that breeds one-sided fixes. Convention check: AGENTS.md Search Rules (lines 179-180) explicitly mandate the split `debounceTask` (delay) + `searchTask` (work) properties, and the canonical SearchViewController (/Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Interface/Search/SearchViewController+Search.swift) follows that split — so the flagged single-task pattern is the opposite of the repo's documented convention; conventionEndorsed=false. The suggestion is also AGENTS-compatible: a shared helper in Interface/Browse/Support matches the documented scope "browse-only helpers shared across album/song/download flows", and canonicalizing to the debounceTask/searchTask split makes the code MORE like the rest of the repo, not less. One overstated detail: `searchTracksByID` vs `tracksByID` is partially structural, not gratuitous — SongsViewController keeps a separate full-library `tracksByID` (SongsViewController.swift line 228, lookup switch at line 277), while SongLibraryViewController only populates `tracksByID` for search results. That nuance does not undermine the core finding. Severity stays medium: the divergent guards and especially the keep-old-results vs clear-to-empty error behavior actively mislead readers about intent.

The didSet replay exists as described, but it is a deliberate, load-bearing design: NowPlayingArtworkImageView starts loading artwork in init (Combine subscription) before the controller binds onArtworkLoaded in bindArtworkSectionActions, so the replay-on-assign is required for the popup item image and background coordinator to receive an already-loaded image. The flagged property is the terminal link of a four-file didSet-forwarding chain (NowPlayingCompactPageController → NowPlayingCenterSectionView → NowPlayingAvatarSectionView → MuseAmpImageView), making assignment-time didSet side effects the dominant deliberate convention of this module. AGENTS.md Property Rules explicitly mandate the non-optional closure-property-with-empty-default form for callbacks, and the NowPlayingArtworkShellPresenting protocol requires onArtworkLoaded as a settable property (NowPlayingRelaxedController implements it as a forwarding computed get/set), so the suggested setOnImageLoaded(_:replayingCurrentImage:) method would break the protocol design and read unlike the rest of the repo. Technically-true naming nit, but the fix is a net negative here.

Confirmed and stronger than claimed. currentTimeline() (Actions.swift lines 90-102) re-derives the timeline from lyricsService.cachedLyrics + re-conversion + re-parse, while the rendered items come from a ParsedLyrics built via loadLyrics in Binding.swift that the view discards (only items[] is kept). LyricsService.loadLyrics only writes to the cache when the track is downloaded (persistLyricsIfDownloaded guards on database.hasTrack), so for any streamed track cachedLyrics returns nil, currentTimeline() returns nil, and seekToLine / "Play from Here" silently no-op despite a synced timeline being displayed — a permanent functional bug, not just a transient race. AGENTS.md does not endorse the pattern; the suggested fix (retain the ParsedLyrics/timeline applied to the snapshot as the single source of truth) matches the file's existing structure and the repo's source-of-truth property rules.

The cited code exists as described: PreviewCoordinator (MuseAmp/Interface/Common/MuseAmpImageView/MuseAmpImageView+Preview.swift lines 79-97) holds three optionals and force-unwraps previewItemURL in previewController(_:previewItemAt:). However, the finding fails on three counts. (1) Dominant local convention: the only other QLPreviewControllerDataSource in the repo, ImageQuickLookPreviewPresenter.swift (same Interface/Common area, same author/date), uses the exact same pattern — optional previewItemURL, count = nil ? 0 : 1, and `previewItemURL! as NSURL`. Rewriting PreviewCoordinator as a state enum would make it read unlike its sibling, which the verification rubric says makes a technically-true finding not real. (2) The enum fix would not actually remove the contract reliance: previewItemAt must return a non-optional QLPreviewItem, so in .empty/.loaded states you would still need a fatalError/precondition/dummy-URL fallback — the `!` is just exchanged for a different explicit failure path guarded by the same numberOfPreviewItems contract, which is the standard idiom for this delegate API. (3) The 'implicit combinations' claim is overstated: the coordinator is 19 lines and mutated at exactly two call sites (handleLoadedImageApplied sets all three together; clear() nils all three), and the 'pending' prefix already communicates the awaiting-materialization state. AGENTS.md Property Rules ('avoid unnecessary optionals... if a property can have a meaningful default value') do not condemn these optionals — nil is the meaningful 'nothing loaded yet' value with no sensible default. The crash scenario (clear() racing a presented QLPreviewController) is the same latent narrowness present in the conventionally-accepted sibling and is not a clarity defect of this class specifically.

Verified in /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Interface/NowPlaying/Sections/NowPlayingQueueSectionView.swift: line 143 declares `var isProgramaticScrollBlocked: Date = .distantPast`, and the misspelled identifier appears at lines 143, 608, 618, 622, 634, 635, while the same file spells `pendingProgrammaticScrollRetry`, `blockProgrammaticScroll`, `programmaticScrollBlockDuration`, and `hasActiveProgrammaticScrollBlock` with the correct double-m. Verified at AlbumTrackCell.swift:199-202: trackNumberImage builds "\(number).circle.fill" with no fallback; numbered SF Symbols only exist for 0-50, so larger track numbers produce a nil image and a silent blank gap. The suggested fallback is a one-line UIKit-idiomatic change that makes the hidden 0-50 limit explicit without violating any repo convention. Severity remains low: rare data shape and purely cosmetic degradation.

Every cited location verifies: LyricTimelineCell (line 5, selectionStyle at 30), LyricTimelineMessageCell (5/25), LyricTimelineSpacerCell (5/12), LyricSelectionCell (LyricSelectionSheetViewController.swift:196) all subclass UITableViewCell directly; NowPlayingQueueSectionView.swift:331 and :715 redundantly set selectionStyle = .none on cells already inheriting TableBaseCell. All three cited locations verified verbatim: NowPlayingCompactController+Playback.swift line 28 (trailing guard ending applySupplementalPlaybackProgress) and lines 88-90 (trailing guard ending refreshPlayingContent), plus NowPlayingRelaxedController+Playback.swift lines 29-31 where the entire body is one guard. Verified all cited locations: NowPlayingCompactController.swift has viewDidAppear (104-106) and viewDidLayoutSubviews (108-110) that only call super plus an empty deinit (127); NowPlayingRelaxedController.swift mirrors this (108-110, 139); LyricTimelineView.swift (actually at Interface/NowPlaying/LyricTimeline/, lines 183-185) and EdgeFadeBlurView.swift (28-30) have layoutSubviews overrides that only call super. Verified all three cited locations exist exactly as described: NowPlayingCompactController+Playback.swift line 28 and lines 88-90 end methods with guards followed by no code, and NowPlayingRelaxedController+Playback.swift lines 29-31 has a body that is only a guard. Verified the finding against both files. /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Interface/NowPlaying/Components/NowPlayingRelaxedShellView.swift:15 declares `private(set) var currentRightPanel: NowPlayingRelaxedPanel = .lyrics` and guards/mutates it in `switchRightPanel` (lines 65-69). /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Interface/NowPlaying/Controller/NowPlayingRelaxedController.swift:69 declares an identical `private(set) var currentRightPanel` and its own `switchRightPanel` (lines 201-213) with an identical guard, then forwards to `relaxedShellView.switchRightPanel`. Verified: line 200 of NowPlayingQueueHeaderCell.swift calls shuffleButton.layer.removeAnimation(forKey: "queueShuffleFade"), and repo-wide grep shows that key string occurs nowhere else — no layer.add ever registers it, so the call is a guaranteed no-op. Confirmed: loadData() (lines 245-271 of MuseAmp/Interface/Sync/SyncContentPickerViewController.swift) runs `try await` inside an unobserved `Task { @MainActor ... }` with no do/catch, so database read errors vanish with no log and no UI feedback. Confirmed: PlaylistCell.swift:14 declares an unbounded NSCache<NSString, UIImage> with NSString-bridged keys at lines 95/110/138, and cache keys embed updatedAt timestamps so entries accumulate across playlist edits. The cited code exists and textually conflicts with the AGENTS.md line, but the finding fails on substance. (a) The suggested fix is behaviorally wrong: in setHighlighted(true) the code sets highlightView.alpha = 1 directly with no animation block, so Interface.animate's .beginFromCurrentState never applies to that path; without removeAllAnimations(), an in-flight fade-out CAAnimation masks the model-layer alpha until it finishes, breaking immediate highlight on quick re-tap. The call is load-bearing, not redundant. (b) The identical pattern is the dominant deliberate convention: AlbumTrackCell.swift lines 163/178 are byte-identical, and the repo ships a shared helper ViewAnimationHelper.removeAnimationsRecursively whose only job is calling removeAllAnimations (also used in MuseAmpImageView and AnimatedTextLabel). Deleting the calls in one cell would make it read and behave unlike its siblings. (c) Per the verifier rubric, a technically-true convention citation whose fix introduces a regression and diverges from surrounding code is not real.

Verified at MuseAmp/Interface/Playlist/PlaylistDetailViewController.swift:238 — the code wraps the non-optional dataSource.snapshot() in Optional() purely to obtain a mutable var binding inside the guard list. Repo-wide grep shows this is the only occurrence of the pattern; every other snapshot-mutation site (including reconfigureSongCells at line 339 in the same file, PlaylistDetailViewController+Actions.swift:161, AlbumDetailViewController.swift:471, MainController+Sidebar.swift:422) uses the plain `guard let dataSource else { return }; var snapshot = dataSource.snapshot()` form. AGENTS.md does not endorse the Optional() trick; its style rules (early returns, guard to reduce nesting) align with the suggested rewrite, which matches the dominant convention. Cosmetic clarity issue only — no behavioral risk — so severity stays low.

Verified at MuseAmp/Interface/Playlist/PlaylistDetailViewController.swift:165-167: the @objc wrapper handlePlaylistsDidChange() does nothing but call handlePlaylistStoreDidChange(), which has no other caller anywhere in the repo (grep confirmed). Both are private, the names are near-synonyms, and the target name is itself handler-shaped, so the hop carries no descriptive payload. The dominant convention in this file and sibling controllers (handleLibraryDidSync, SongsViewController, AlbumDetailViewController, TabBarController) is @objc handlers that either inline the body or delegate to a behavior-named method used by multiple callers — never a synonym wrapper. AGENTS.md does not endorse this pattern; the suggested merge keeps the selector working and matches local style. Severity stays low: it is a one-hop cosmetic indirection that does not mislead or breed bugs.

Confirmed at MuseAmp/Interface/Playlist/PlaylistDetailViewController+Menu.swift lines 49-62: Rename UIAction is built unconditionally, then removed for liked-songs playlists by matching the localized title string. This is the only such post-hoc-removal-by-title pattern in the repo; the dominant convention — in the same file, buildCoverMenu (lines 119-129) — is conditional append (`if canRegenerateCover { children.append(...) }`), so the suggested fix aligns with local style. AGENTS.md contains nothing endorsing title-based filtering and elsewhere discourages logic keyed on user-visible `.title` text. The fragility is real: both call sites share a string literal, so changing the title at line 50 without updating the filter at line 60 silently breaks the removal and leaks Rename into the liked-songs menu. The locale-collation sub-claim is overstated (both sides resolve through the same localization key), but the core hazard and split-logic readability cost stand. Medium severity: actively breeds a silent regression and forces readers to scan two locations to learn when Rename appears.

Every cited duplicate verified by reading all four controllers: addSectionHeader/refreshUI byte-identical in 4 files (only occurrences in the repo, so not a repo-wide deliberate convention), popToRoleSelection byte-identical in 2, canScanQRCode identical in 2, openQRScannerIfAvailable near-identical in 2, handleBackgroundInterruption identical in 2 (the third, in SyncTransferProgressViewController, is intentionally different). The signature drift is real and hazardous: SyncServerStatusViewController.addInfoView takes value: String while the other two controllers take value: String.LocalizationValue plus a rawValue: String overload, so the same literal call shape localizes in two screens but not the third — a copy-paste trap that conflicts with the repo's strict Localization Rules. AGENTS.md does not endorse the duplication; its placement rules (keep feature-only shared UI inside the feature folder) are satisfied by the suggested Interface/Sync/-local shared extension/base, and the repo already uses shared bases (TableBaseCell), so the fix reads like the rest of the codebase. Severity adjusted from high to medium: no bug has actually shipped yet (all value: call sites in SyncServerStatusViewController correctly wrap literals in String(localized:)), but the drift actively breeds localization bugs and forces 2-4x edits for any behavior fix in these helpers.

Both factual claims verified: line 113 inlines [16, 25, 36, 64] and createRandomPlaylist(count: Int = 25) at line 255 has a dead default (private func, sole caller at line 116 always passes count). However, the headline suggestion to hoist a named randomPlaylistSizes constant is refuted by the dominant local convention: DownloadsViewController.swift:217 builds the identical kind of count-options menu from an inline (1 ... 8).map — both existing count menus in the repo use inline literals mapped directly to titled actions, so the values are self-describing in context and hoisting would diverge from sibling code. AGENTS.md neither mandates nor forbids either style. What keeps the finding real is the unused `= 25` default: it is verified dead code that duplicates a menu literal and mildly misleads readers into thinking a canonical default exists; dropping it is a trivial, convention-neutral fix. Net: partially real, cosmetic only — severity low; apply only the drop-the-default portion of the suggestion.

The duplication is real as stated (five identical name tiebreaks in PlaylistViewController.applySort, lines 398-428), but the inline-comparator-per-branch style is the dominant deliberate convention for every applySort in the repo — SongsViewController (356-389), SongLibraryViewController (381-410), and SyncContentPickerViewController all repeat their tiebreak comparator inline with no extracted helper. Extracting a nameAscending helper in only this controller would make it read unlike its direct siblings, and the suggestion's benefit ('collation rule lives in one place') is illusory since the same rule stays duplicated in 4+ other files regardless. Technically true, but the fix diverges from established module style for marginal gain, so per the repository-conventions principle the finding is not real.

Verified: updateSearchResults lines 16-21 and scheduleSearch's guard lines 34-40 duplicate the exact 5-step reset (cancel task, clear currentQuery, searchResultsByID, orderedSearchResultIDs, applyPlaylistsSnapshot). The suggested fix is provably behavior-preserving: scheduleSearch cancels searchTask and runs its empty/whitespace guard synchronously before any debounce Task is created, so unconditional delegation resets immediately with the same animated:true default. The if-branch is fully redundant (whitespace-only queries already reach the guard). AGENTS.md does not endorse the duplication; SearchViewController+Search.swift in the same repo already uses the unconditional-delegation shape, so the fix matches existing repo style. Severity stays low: blocks are adjacent in a small file and no bug has yet resulted.

The cited code exists at line 72 of /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Interface/Playlist/PlaylistDetailViewController+Actions.swift (`albumID: albumID ?? "unknown"`), but the finding mischaracterizes it as a call-site-local magic string. "unknown" is the deliberate, dominant repo-wide sentinel for an unresolved album ID: (1) /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Backend/Library/EmbeddedMetadataReader.swift:74 uses the identical `albumID ?? "unknown"` pattern; (2) MuseAmpDatabaseKit's public API bakes it in as a default parameter (`inferredRelativePath(for:albumID: String = "unknown"...)` in LibraryPaths.swift:94) and its StringUtilities sanitizer returns "unknown" as fallback; (3) the consumer side is already centralized behind a named semantic helper, `String.isKnownAlbumID` in /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Backend/Supplement/StringUtilities.swift (`!isEmpty && self != "unknown"`), which undercuts the claim that downstream handling is invisible and that consumers must repeat the bare string. The suggested fix (a named constant next to SongDownloadRequest, or an optional albumID owned by downloadRequest) cannot unify the sentinel anyway because MuseAmpDatabaseKit produces the same literal independently, and changing only this one call site would make it read unlike its sibling producer in EmbeddedMetadataReader. A broader cross-package refactor is beyond the scope of this clarity finding. Per the repository-conventions principle, a technically-true observation whose fix diverges from the surrounding module's dominant convention is not a real finding.

The duplication is factually present (PlaylistViewController.swift:432-462, two near-identical snapshot methods), but the paired self-contained apply<Domain>Snapshot/applySearchSnapshot structure is the dominant deliberate convention across the module: SongsViewController.swift:324-342 and SongLibraryViewController.swift:325-343 use the exact same parallel-pair shape under the same '// MARK: - Snapshots' header. AGENTS.md's View Lifecycle Rules prescribe this per-controller recipe ('Track a hasAppliedInitialSnapshot flag per controller', 'Use snapshot.reconfigureItems() for in-place cell updates'), and the duplicated bodies are direct instantiations of it. Collapsing one controller's pair into a parameterized helper would make PlaylistViewController read unlike its sibling controllers while leaving the same duplication in two other files, so the fix violates the repository-conventions principle for marginal benefit. The two methods are adjacent, ~14 lines each, and easy to keep in sync.

Verified against the file: reorderSections (lines 135-153) contains exactly the cited semicolon-packed lines, single-letter names (tb, sa, sb, l, n, a, b), and the force unwrap tb[a]!. Confirmed SearchSection.resultSections = [.albums, .songs], so the .loading branches and tb's .loading entry are dead code, and tb[a]! is a latent crash if resultSections gains a case not mirrored in tb (dictionary completeness is not compiler-checked, unlike the switches). Confirmed: PlaylistDetailViewController.swift:301 and :327 set selectionStyle = .none on PlaylistHeaderCell and DetailFooterCell, both of which inherit TableBaseCell (MuseAmp/Interface/Common/TableBaseCell.swift), whose init already sets selectionStyle = .none and whose setEditing owns the toggling. AGENTS.md Cell Rules explicitly state cells inherit TableBaseCell which sets selectionStyle = .none and 'Never use selectionStyle to remove the gray tap highlight' — so the documented convention endorses removing these lines, not keeping them. Deleting them is behavior-neutral: header/footer rows are not editable (canEditRowAt returns false for non-song items), and during editing the table's setEditing call runs after the cell provider, overriding the assignment anyway. The same redundant pattern appears in a few other controllers, but a repeated violation does not become a convention when AGENTS.md explicitly mandates the opposite. Severity remains low: pure redundancy/cargo-cult risk, no actual bug produced.

All three cited occurrences exist exactly as described (SearchViewController+Search.swift:41 and :64, SearchViewController.swift:269). Verified in MuseAmp/Interface/Settings/SettingsViewController.swift: refreshDownloadsExplain (lines 102-108) and makeDownloadsObject (lines 113-120) duplicate both the ByteCountFormatter call on environment.downloadStore.localLibraryStorageSize() and the literal "Download queue, progress, and local storage.\nStored Locally: \(storedSize)". Verified: historyTableView uses classic dataSource (SearchViewController.swift:146) with manual deleteRows (SearchViewController+Table.swift:142) and reloadData (lines 205, 211) over the searchHistory array. Verified in MuseAmp/Interface/Playlist/PlaylistViewController+Table.swift lines 158-170: targetedPreview derives the row from sortedPlaylists.firstIndex and hand-builds IndexPath(row:, section: 0), bypassing the diffable data source. During active search the snapshot (applySearchSnapshot, PlaylistViewController.swift:448-462) contains only the .searchResults section ordered by orderedSearchResultIDs (filtered store.playlists order, not sortedPlaylists order), and context menus are not disabled while searching (contextMenuConfigurationForRowAt guards only !isEditing and handles .searchResult items), so the derived row points at the wrong cell or returns nil. The suggested fix (dataSource.indexPath(for:)) matches the dominant repo convention: sibling controllers (SongsViewController+Table, DownloadsViewController, AlbumDetailViewController+Table) resolve previews via CellContextMenuPreviewHelper using snapshot-consistent index paths, and AlbumDetailViewController.swift:605 already uses dataSource.indexPath(for:). AGENTS.md explicitly forbids stored parallel state duplicating an existing source of truth and mandates diffable data sources, so the pattern is the opposite of endorsed. Severity stays medium: the bug is live but its consequence is a cosmetic preview-animation glitch in search mode, not data loss.

Verified in LogViewerController.swift: line 296 assigns parseLogLines() to `allLines`, but parseLogLines (307-342) filters by selectedLevels (333) and selectedCategories (334-336) and mutates allCategories (340) as a side effect. Verified in SearchViewController.swift (SearchSection lines 16-22, SearchItem.showMore line 27, applySnapshot 262-264) and SearchViewController+Search.swift (loadMore guard line 156 plus dead case .loading returns at 169-170 and 190-191, reorderSections -1 sentinel at 149-150). Confirmed: SearchViewController.applySnapshot (lines ~277-292) builds a fresh UIView container + UILabel with SnapKit constraints and installs it as tableView.backgroundView every time searchError is set with no results, tearing it down by nil-ing backgroundView. AGENTS.md View Lifecycle Rules explicitly mandate the opposite ("Pre-alloc subviews and toggle isHidden instead of creating/destroying views during data updates"), and the dominant feature-controller convention (PlaylistViewController, SongsViewController, SongLibraryViewController, DownloadsViewController) uses pre-allocated stored EmptyStateView properties toggled via isHidden. The only similar rebuild pattern elsewhere is LogViewerController in Settings, a minority outlier, not an endorsed convention. The suggested fix (stored error view, update text + isHidden in applySnapshot) aligns the code with both AGENTS.md and sibling screens. Severity adjusted to low: the rebuild is idempotent, fires only in the rare error-with-no-results state, has not bred a bug (no stale state or leak), and the issue is a convention/abstraction-level deviation rather than something that actively misleads readers.

Verified directly in MuseAmp/Interface/Settings/LogViewerController.swift: line 12 conforms to UITableViewDataSource, line 159 wires dataSource = self, reloadData() at lines 93/103/303, and lines 117-135 dequeue a raw UITableViewCell with cell.selectionStyle = .none. AGENTS.md (View Lifecycle Rules line 124, Cell Rules lines 140-141) explicitly mandates diffable data sources for all tables, TableBaseCell inheritance for all cells, and forbids selectionStyle as the highlight-suppression mechanism — three direct violations. The pattern is not the dominant local convention: 10 Interface files use diffable data sources (Browse, Playlist, Search, Downloads, Sync, LyricSheet) versus only 4 raw data-source holdouts, and no AGENTS.md exception covers the log viewer. The suggested fix (diffable keyed by a hashable LogLine identity + a TableBaseCell subclass) would make the file read more like the rest of the repo, not less. Severity stays medium: it is a contained debug screen, but it contradicts hard "must/never" rules in a way future contributors may copy, and per-keystroke reloadData + scrollToBottom is precisely the behavior the diffable mandate exists to prevent.

Verified: PlaylistDetailViewController.swift:124 calls tableView.reloadData() in the cleanSongTitleKey ConfigurableKit sink on a diffable-managed table, while the adjacent playback sink (line 131) correctly uses reconfigureSongCells() (defined line 337, which does snapshot.reconfigureItems on song items with animatingDifferences: false). Confirmed at MuseAmp/Interface/Settings/ServerProfileImportCoordinator.swift. The injectable closure `validateConfiguration` (line 16, default at lines 26-44) persists via AppPreferences.setSubsonicConfiguration before pinging and rolls back/clears on failure — it is an apply-with-rollback, not a validator. `validateAndPersistServerProfile` (lines 221-228) has no persist step; the 'AndPersist' behavior is secretly delegated to the 'validate' closure, and the success alert claims the profile 'was imported and verified'. Both current call sites (SettingsViewController.swift:17, MainController.swift:146) use the default and no tests inject the seam, so no bug has fired yet, but any injector honoring the advertised pure-validator contract would silently break persistence while the UI reports success. AGENTS.md endorses DI/closure seams but not misleading seam names; sibling closures (onConfigurationChanged, onImportCompleted) are accurately named, so the fix fits local convention. Medium: actively misleads readers and breeds bugs at the seam, but nothing has broken yet.

Confirmed verbatim duplication: popToRoleSelection (SyncTransferProgressViewController.swift:368-377) and SyncServerStatusViewController.swift:415-424 are byte-identical, as are the two makeDoneObject builders (346-355 vs 404-413), including identical strings, icon, and MainActor action closure. Verified in /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmpTV/Backend/Sync/TVSessionStateAdapter.swift: the identical disconnect heuristic appears twice — lines 404-407 (`downloadFailureCount > 3, missingEntries.count > 0, Double(downloadFailureCount) / Double(missingEntries.count) > 0.5`) and lines 576-579 (same shape with `resumeFailureCount`/`remainingEntries`). Verified by reading both files: makeRuntimeDependencies and the logSink switch in MuseAmpTV/Application/TVAppContext+Bootstrap.swift are verbatim copies of MuseAmp/Application/AppEnvironment+Bootstrap.swift, differing only in the NSError domain string. The file is TV-native (regular file), while 47 other shared files in MuseAmpTV/ — including Backend/Library, Playback, Supplement, Logging, API, and even Application/AppPreferences.swift — are relative symlinks, the mechanism AGENTS.md explicitly documents (line 100) for code the TV target must compile. Confirmed at MuseAmpTV/Application/TVAppContext+Session.swift:137-146: in the .valid autoPlay branch, the `if !started` block returns true, so the trailing `return started` is only reachable when started == true — every path returns true. The conditional-looking return falsely implies the Bool reflects playback success, while all three callers (TVSessionStateAdapter.swift:328/461/643) bind it as `didSaveSession`, i.e. "session persisted." Confirmed in MuseAmpTV/Backend/Sync/TVSessionStateAdapter.swift: transferReadyContinuation (line 52) is suspended on via plain withCheckedContinuation in runTransfer (280-282) and resumed only by proceedWithTransfer() (217-220). cancelTransfer (222-233), retryTransfer (95), connect (193/204), and resetSession (236) cancel transferTask but never resume/nil the continuation; Task cancellation does not resume a bare checked continuation, so the suspended task becomes a permanent zombie, and a later connect() overwrites the property at line 281 without resuming the orphaned continuation — one leaked task per cancel/reconnect cycle in a normal user flow. Not convention-endorsed: AGENTS.md (line 222) explicitly prefers the shared ContinuationOnceGuard, and the only other stored continuation in the repo (SyncBonjourBrowser.NetServiceResolver) resumes-and-nils on every completion path. Minor caveat: ContinuationOnceGuard is currently typed for throwing continuations (<Value, any Error>) while this one is <Void, Never>, so the helper needs a small extension, but the core fix (resume-and-nil pending continuation in cancel/retry/connect before cancelling or reassigning) is valid, improves clarity, and matches repo style. Medium severity: latent resource leak plus a second untied one-shot lifecycle state that misleads maintainers of the cancel paths, though stale-resume side effects are guarded by the Task.isCancelled check at line 283.

Verified in MuseAmpTV/Application/TVAppContext+Session.swift. Lines 66-69 apply setRepeatMode(.queue) after didStartPlayback, while playCurrentSession (lines 163-165) already applies it on success, so the rule runs twice on the fresh-play path and its ownership is split; the outer call is only needed for the restored-playback branch (restorePersistedPlaybackIfNeeded restores persisted state). AGENTS.md does not endorse the pattern, and the suggested fix (single owner for the repeat rule plus if/else) aligns with the repo's stated style (early returns, reduced nesting) and surrounding code. Verified the substance. /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmpTV/Application/TVAppContext+Session.swift:14 contains `private static let generatedPairingCode: String = .init(format: "%06d", Int.random(in: 0 ..< 1_000_000))`, which is the exact expression in SyncPasswordGenerator.generate() (/Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmp/Backend/Sync/SyncPasswordGenerator.swift:12). The generator is genuinely available to the TV target: MuseAmpTV/Backend/Sync/SyncPasswordGenerator.swift is a relative symlink to the canonical file, the project uses fileSystemSynchronizedGroups (so symlinked files compile into the TV target), and the same file already uses another symlinked Sync type (SyncBonjourIdentity) at lines 15-28, proving the pattern works. Semantically the pairing code IS the sync password: SyncPlaylistAppleTVSenderViewController.swift:325 passes `selectedReceiverInfo?.pairingCode` directly as the `password:` parameter of the handshake, and SyncTransferSession uses SyncPasswordGenerator.generate() for the same field on the iOS side. So the 6-digit format is a cross-device protocol contract now defined in two places — if one side changed the format, pairing-code entry/display could silently diverge. AGENTS.md does not endorse inline duplication; on the contrary, its symlink convention for MuseAmpTV/Backend/Sync exists precisely so shared Sync logic has one canonical owner, and the suggested fix (call SyncPasswordGenerator.generate()) matches how SyncTransferSession already does it, so the fix reads exactly like the rest of the repo. Severity stays low: it is a one-line, currently-stable format and no bug exists today.

Confirmed: TVSceneDelegate.swift:42 calls UIView.transition directly, violating AGENTS.md:133 ("Never call UIView.animate or UIView.transition directly"). No tvOS exemption exists; the TV target's dominant convention is Interface wrappers (15+ call sites), and the only two direct UIView.transition calls in MuseAmpTV exist because the TV-local Interface enum (MuseAmpTV/Interface/Common/Interface.swift) lacks a transition wrapper. The iOS twin performs the same window-root swap via Interface.transition (SceneDelegate.swift:77 / Interface.swift:91). The risk the rule guards against has already materialized: the other direct call (TVLyricTimelineView.swift:203) drops .beginFromCurrentState. The suggested fix (add a transition wrapper mirroring iOS and route the call through it) aligns with both AGENTS.md and local style.

Verified: TVAppContext+Session.swift:14-15 stores per-launch random pairing identity (pairing code, Bonjour token) as `private static let` in an extension, with static computed advertised names; instance accessors just forward Self statics. Contrast claim confirmed — SyncTransferSession.swift keeps the same kind of identity as instance vars and regenerates them in prepareSender (line 111). AGENTS.md does not endorse static/type-level state; it explicitly discourages singletons and prefers DI, and every other `static let` in MuseAmpTV is a true constant (layout/fonts), so this random identity masquerades as the constants convention. Mitigating: only one TVAppContext exists per process (TVBootProgressController) and no tests instantiate it, so the shared-identity hazard is hypothetical today — but the static makes pairing-identity rotation impossible without restructuring, diverging from the established instance-level pattern. The suggested fix is behavior-preserving and convention-aligned.

All three claims verified against the code. (1) AMTVUploadWaitingContent's only consumer is TVQRPairingView.configure(content:), which reads just qrPayload and message; title/deviceName/connectionCodeTitle/connectionCode are never rendered anywhere (single call site at TVRootViewController.swift:86). (2) The adapter's .connecting variant ("Connecting to Sender", "Selected Sender", authenticating message) is dead — during connecting the visible UI is a separate UIAlertController in TVRootViewController.beginConnection, and the pairing view's instructionLabel is hardcoded at TVQRPairingView.swift:34 with the byte-identical localized string the adapter emits at TVSessionStateAdapter.swift:158, so the copy is duplicated and the label never reflects content.message. (3) Lines 106-113 set isAccessibilityElement = false then assign accessibilityTraits/accessibilityLabel, which VoiceOver ignores on a non-element container — the assembled label is dead work. AGENTS.md does not endorse the pattern; it cuts against it (remove unused localized strings, thin TV adapters, derive state rather than duplicate), and the sibling TVTransferProgressView.configure consumes its content struct fully, so the deviation is not local convention either. The suggested fix (render content.message or shrink the struct and delete the dead adapter strings, plus resolving the accessibility contradiction) improves clarity without violating any repo rule. Medium severity: a maintainer editing the adapter's message would expect the screen to change and it silently won't, and duplicated localized copy risks divergence under the repo's xcstrings hygiene rules; no shipped functional bug beyond the inert accessibility label, so not high.

Verified: interactionSubject, focusSubject, and var userInteractionDeadline (lines 64-66 of MuseAmpTV/Interface/NowPlaying/Lyrics/TVLyricTimelineView.swift) are internal but have zero consumers outside the file; the only external reference to the class (TVNowPlayingView.swift) never touches them. Marking them private compiles since all usage, including the same-file UITableViewDataSource/Delegate extension, is file-local. The iOS sibling LyricTimelineView uses internal access only because its logic is split across separate-file extensions (+Binding.swift, +Delegate.swift), a necessity that does not apply to this single-file tvOS class — so that is not an endorsing convention. Within this file the dominant style is private (tableView, items, parsedLyrics, cancellables, etc.), making these three the local anomaly; the fix matches surrounding style and violates nothing in AGENTS.md. Severity stays low: purely an encapsulation/clarity cosmetic; no current misuse exists.

Verified: isMacDevice(_:) at lines 184-192 of MuseAmpTV/Interface/Pairing/TVQRPairingView.swift is a private static func with exactly one occurrence in the entire repo (its own definition, confirmed by grep). Being private, no external caller is possible, and nothing in the file invokes it. The equivalent substring checks are duplicated inline in symbolName(for:) at lines 211-222, which is the version actually used (line 156). AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md does not endorse keeping unused helpers; deleting it is a zero-risk clarity win consistent with repo style. Cosmetic only, so severity stays low.

Confirmed at /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmpTV/Application/TVAppContext.swift line 36: `Date().timeIntervalSince(lastUserInteractionDate) < 5.0` inlines the interaction-window duration. The surrounding property name (`isWithinUserInteractionWindow`) softens but does not eliminate the issue: the 5-second policy value itself is unnamed and undocumented. The repository's dominant deliberate convention is named TimeInterval constants for exactly this kind of cooldown/window value — e.g. PlaybackController.periodicPlaybackStatusLogInterval, LyricTimelineView.Constants.autoScrollCooldown/userInteractionCooldown, and in the same tvOS target TVLyricTimelineView's userInteractionCooldown: TimeInterval = 1.0. AGENTS.md does not endorse inline durations (its only hardcoded-value rule, 200 pt spacers, is scoped to lyrics/queue spacers). The suggested `private static let userInteractionWindow: TimeInterval = 5` would read like the rest of the repo, not against it. Severity is cosmetic since the property name carries most of the intent, so low is correct.

Verified: AGENTS.md:100 documents symlink mirroring only for MuseAmpTV/Backend/Sync/, but find -type l shows 47 symlinks spanning Application/AppPreferences.swift and nine Backend subtrees (API, Downloads, Library, Logging, Lyrics, Playback, Playlist, Supplement, Sync). The only non-symlinked Backend files are Models/* and Sync/TVSessionStateAdapter.swift, exactly as the suggestion states. No other doc covers the mirroring (grep across AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/README.md), and CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md. The gap dates to the init commit. The doc's Documentation Sync rule mandates accuracy here rather than endorsing the omission, and the project's responsibility-split convention (e.g., five separate PlaybackController+*.swift symlinks) makes the predicted failure concrete: adding a non-Sync shared backend file without a TV symlink breaks make build-tvos, with no instruction telling an agent to create one. The suggested fix is factually accurate and improves the doc without conflicting with any convention. Medium severity: actively misleads and breeds build breaks, but failures are loud compile errors, not silent corruption.

Verified: TVLyricTimelineView.swift:203-209 calls UIView.transition directly with [.transitionCrossDissolve, .allowUserInteraction], omitting .beginFromCurrentState. AGENTS.md Animation Rules (line 133) explicitly forbid calling UIView.transition directly and mandate Interface wrapper methods including `transition`. The iOS counterpart (MuseAmp/Interface/NowPlaying/LyricTimeline/LyricTimelineView.swift) routes the identical crossfade through Interface.transition. The TV target has its own Interface enum (MuseAmpTV/Interface/Common/Interface.swift) with interruptibleOptions and animate/smoothSpringAnimate wrappers — used elsewhere in this very file (Interface.animate at line 178) — but no transition wrapper yet; this and TVSceneDelegate.swift:42 are the only two direct UIView.transition calls in the TV target, so the deviation is not a deliberate local convention. The suggested fix aligns with both AGENTS.md and the dominant style. Severity stays low: cosmetic convention drift; the missing .beginFromCurrentState on a brief crossfade reload is unlikely to have produced a user-visible bug.

Verified end-to-end: AMTVReceivingTracksContent.title and .progress are populated by TVSessionStateAdapter (phase-specific localized titles at lines 170/180; fractional progress math in two download-progress closures) but the sole consumer, TVTransferProgressView.configure (line 59), recomputes its own generic title from sourceDeviceName and never reads progress (view only has an indeterminate spinner). receivingTracksContent's only call site is TVRootViewController.swift:100, so both fields are fully dead. This actively misleads: editing the adapter's titles or progress math has zero visible effect, and the adapter ships never-displayed localized strings. AGENTS.md does not endorse the pattern (its Property Rules discourage dead derived state); the similar partial-field discard in TVQRPairingView is the same defect repeated, not a deliberate convention. The suggested fix fits repo style.

Cited code confirmed: TVLyricTimelineView.swift uses classic UITableViewDataSource (line 375), dataSource/delegate = self (lines 80-81), manual reloadData() (lines 207/215/233), and hand-rolled structure diffing (lines 221-229), while AGENTS.md line 124 unconditionally mandates diffable data sources with no documented exception. However, the pattern is the dominant deliberate convention of the surrounding NowPlaying module: iOS LyricTimelineView, NowPlayingQueueSectionView, SearchViewController history table, and LogViewerController all use classic data sources, and the TV file mirrors the iOS lyric timeline including its performance-tuned per-cell active-state update path. So conventionEndorsed=true via local convention, but AGENTS.md itself explicitly contradicts the code rather than endorsing it. The finding is real because it accurately identifies rule/code drift, acknowledges the established exception, and offers a documentation-only fix (record the lyric-timeline exception in AGENTS.md) that resolves the contradiction without making any code read unlike the rest of the repo. Severity stays low: the harm is misleading documentation for maintainers, not a bug or blocked modification.

Verified in MuseAmpTV/Interface/Common/TVTransportButton.swift: unfocused alpha 0.1 appears at lines 29, 56, 75, 83; the ternary `self.alpha = self.isFocused ? 1.0 : 0.1` is duplicated verbatim in didUpdateFocus, pressesEnded, and pressesCancelled; pressed alpha 0.5 at line 65. AGENTS.md does not endorse inline literals, and the surrounding tvOS module already uses named design constants (TVNowPlayingLayout, which this file references for transportButtonSize), so private alpha constants plus a restingAlpha helper would match local convention. The issue is real but cosmetic — all sites are in one small file — so severity remains low.

Confirmed: TVRootBackgroundView.extractColors (lines 119-184) inlines a full dominant-color algorithm with seven unnamed tuning constants (64 sample size, stride 4, brightness 60..700, saturation 20, /32 quantization, prefix(4)) inside a UIView that otherwise only orchestrates gradient/cache state. AGENTS.md does not endorse this — it explicitly wants MuseAmpTV/Interface/Root/ kept to thin shell/adapter wiring. The repo's dominant convention for the identical task is NowPlayingArtworkBackgroundCoordinator (iOS), which delegates to DominantColors with semantic options (.excludeBlack/.excludeWhite/.excludeGray, maxCount: 4); the TV code re-implements those exclusions as raw magic numbers that must be reverse-engineered. Extracting a named-constant palette helper would converge with, not diverge from, repo style. Severity lowered to low: the method is private, contiguous, and decodable by a careful reader — it is opaque rather than actively misleading, and has not bred a bug.

All four cited stored-state clusters exist at the exact lines claimed (8-24, 59, 257-258, 303) in the 463-line TVRootViewController.swift. The file is internally inconsistent: hasPerformedSlideToProgress/previousState are at top while didResumePlaybackAfterBoot, lastBackgroundTrackID/playbackWasActive, and connectingAlert are scattered mid-file, so this is not a deliberate colocate-with-usage style. The repo's dominant convention (TVNowPlayingController, TVSessionStateAdapter, PlaylistViewController) declares all stored properties at the top with MARK sections reserved for behavior; AGENTS.md neither mandates nor endorses mid-file property declarations. The suggested fix aligns the file with surrounding code and violates nothing. Severity stays low: it slows auditing the controller's state set but does not mislead or breed bugs.

Confirmed: encoder format "tv-session|sessionID|index|trackID" lives in MuseAmp/Backend/Sync/TVPlaylistSessionStore.swift:58-59 (symlinked into MuseAmpTV/Backend/Sync/), while the inverse is hand-rolled identically twice in MuseAmpTV/Interface/NowPlaying/TVNowPlayingController.swift (lines 25 and 176) with no canonical decode helper anywhere in the repo. The ad-hoc parse is also subtly fragile (trackIDs containing "|" would be truncated; format changes break silently with no compile-time linkage). AGENTS.md does not endorse the pattern — it places transport helpers under Backend/Sync/ and the suggested decode helper next to playbackItemIdentifier fits the module's existing style and the existing symlink mirroring rule, so the fix improves clarity without violating conventions.

Confirmed: TVRootBackgroundView.swift:32 declares `private var colorCache: [String: [UIColor]] = [:]`, populated per distinct artwork URL (line 85) with no eviction, on a root view that lives for the app lifetime. AGENTS.md line 274 explicitly prescribes "Use LRUCache for bounded in-memory caches", and the iOS counterpart of this exact pattern (MuseAmp/Interface/NowPlaying/Support/NowPlayingArtworkBackgroundCoordinator.swift:24) uses `LRUCache<String, [UIColor]>(countLimit: 32)` — so LRUCache is both the written rule and the dominant in-repo convention for this cache shape; the dictionary is the deviation, not the local norm. One caveat the suggestion omits: the MuseAmpTV target does not currently link the LRUCache package product (project.pbxproj packageProductDependencies), so the fix also needs the existing package product added to the TV target — trivial, since the package is already in the project. Practical memory impact is negligible (a few hundred bytes per distinct artwork), so this is a consistency/convention issue rather than a bug: severity low.

Confirmed: TVNowPlayingContentView.swift lines 54 and 61 hardcode `make.height.equalTo(128)` for two duplicated anonymous spacers. TVNowPlayingLayout.swift is explicitly documented as the centralized constants enum for this page ("All spacing values follow an 8/16/32/64 scale") and is used 30 times across the module, including in this same file (spacing32, line 26). The module even names its other spacer height (lyricSpacerHeight = 200), so naming spacer constants IS the local convention. TVNowPlayingView.swift line 42 derives the same 128 as spacing64 * 2, an implicit coupling the fix would make explicit. AGENTS.md's "hardcoded 200 pt" rule covers a different spacer (iOS lyrics/queue list) and forbids viewport-fraction math, not named constants — it does not endorse a bare 128 literal here. The suggested fix matches surrounding style exactly, so the finding is real at low severity (cosmetic clarity/duplication risk).

Confirmed: the 10-second seek step is duplicated at lines 191 and 198 and the 0.5-second scrub-commit cooldown literal at lines 192 and 203 of MuseAmpTV/Interface/NowPlaying/TVNowPlayingController.swift, none named. The fix matches the repo's dominant convention rather than violating it: the iOS counterpart ProgressTrackView.swift declares `private static let scrubCommitCooldown: TimeInterval = 0.5` and uses it via addingTimeInterval, and the tvOS target itself names similar cooldowns (TVLyricTimelineView's userInteractionCooldown). AGENTS.md does not endorse inline timing literals (its hardcoded-200pt rule covers only lyric/queue spacers). Duplication of the unnamed seek step also risks divergent edits between the two handlers, but impact is still cosmetic, so severity remains low.

Verified in MuseAmpTV/Interface/Root/TVRootViewController.swift: all four sibling alert presenters (lines 349, 362, 378, 420) guard presentedViewController == nil, but presentStatusAlert (397-405) does not. Its sole caller presentPendingSessionAlertIfNeeded (407) consumes the alert via context.takePendingSessionAlert() before presenting, and TVAppContext.takePendingSessionAlert() (TVAppContext.swift:129-142) nils out the stored alert, so a refused present permanently loses it. Concrete collision paths exist: reloadContent presents the .failed alert (144-150) then calls presentPendingSessionAlertIfNeeded (152-154) in the same pass; the connectingAlert (303-312) or Menu-button confirmations can be up when reloadContent fires from the playback snapshot sink (286). Pending alerts carry real user-facing failures (e.g. 'Playback Failed' set in TVAppContext+Session.swift:140 on a path where transferPhase becomes .completed, so only this mechanism surfaces it). The suggested fix (guard before takePendingSessionAlert) is one line, matches the file's dominant guard convention, and violates nothing in AGENTS.md (AlertController rules apply to the iOS target's styled alerts; the tvOS shell consistently uses UIAlertController). Medium severity: silently drops user-facing failure feedback and the inconsistency misleads maintainers, but the collision window is narrow.

Confirmed in MuseAmpTV/Interface/NowPlaying/TVNowPlayingLyricsCoordinator.swift: the memory-cache branch (lines 47-60) and stored-lyrics branch (lines 62-76) are structurally identical except for one cache-write line, and both assign `lyricsTask = Task {...}` without first calling `lyricsTask?.cancel()`, unlike the fetch path (line 83). This both leaks an in-flight fetch for the previous track and drops its handle so clearDisplayedLyrics()/deinit can no longer cancel it; only the shouldApplyLoadedLyrics guard prevents a stale UI write. The iOS original (MuseAmp/Interface/NowPlaying/Support/NowPlayingLyricsCoordinator.swift) updates the view synchronously in its cache branches and never touches lyricsTask there, so the TV pattern is a port artifact, not a module convention. AGENTS.md does not endorse the pattern; its guard/early-return and helper-extraction style is compatible with the suggested applyCachedLyrics(_:for:) extraction. Severity stays low: the duplication is cosmetic and the cancellation gap is defended by the guard (worst case a wasted fetch that warms the cache).

Confirmed in MuseAmpTV/Interface/NowPlaying/Lyrics/TVLyricEdgeFadeView.swift: lines 33-35 override layoutSubviews only to call super (true dead code), and line 45 overrides traitCollectionDidChange with an empty body and no comment. The finding's inference about intent is verified, not speculative: the iOS counterpart MuseAmp/Interface/NowPlaying/Components/EdgeFadeBlurView.swift (which the TV file's header says it mirrors) has the identical empty traitCollectionDidChange WITH a comment explaining the super call is intentionally skipped because it can crash while reconfiguring the private backdrop filter. The TV copy dropped that comment, leaving exactly the ambiguity AGENTS.md says comments exist to remove ('Keep comments rare and only where they remove real ambiguity'). The suggested fix (delete dead layoutSubviews, add the one-line rationale comment) makes the TV file read MORE like the repo's own established pattern, since the only sibling using this trick carries the comment. Not convention-endorsed: AGENTS.md doesn't sanction empty overrides, and the dominant local convention includes the explanatory comment. Severity kept at low: cosmetic dead code plus a missing comment whose risk is partly mitigated by the file header pointing readers to the documented iOS sibling.

Confirmed: diff of the four TV files against MuseAmp/Interface/NowPlaying/LyricTimeline/Lyrics/{LyricParser,LyricTimeline,LyricLine,LyricProgress}.swift after a mechanical TVLyric->Lyric rename is empty (194 duplicated lines, pure Foundation, no UIKit dependency). The repo's dominant cross-target sharing convention is relative symlinks — MuseAmpTV/Backend/Sync/ holds ten symlinks into MuseAmp/Backend/Sync/, and MuseAmpTV/Backend/Lyrics/LyricsService.swift is already a symlink — and AGENTS.md explicitly scopes Backend/Lyrics as 'lyrics fetching, parsing, and Chinese script conversion', so the suggested move+symlink fix matches documented conventions rather than fighting them. No convention endorses the fork-and-rename pattern. Severity adjusted to medium: the copies are still byte-identical, so no divergence bug has actually occurred yet; the harm is a misleading TV-specific name on shared logic and a silent divergence trap for future LRC fixes.

Verified: TVAppContext+Bootstrap.swift:136-144 defines configureImageRequestAuthorization, whose body only filters out any existing .requestModifier from KingfisherManager.shared.defaultOptions and appends .backgroundDecode. No request modifier or authorization is ever installed — a repo-wide grep shows the only .requestModifier references are these two filter sites (TV and iOS), so the function actually removes auth-capable modifiers rather than configuring authorization. The iOS namesake (AppEnvironment+Bootstrap.swift:131) additionally sets Kingfisher cache limits, also unrelated to authorization. git log -S shows the name has been wrong since the init commit (no vestigial auth code it once wrapped that justifies keeping the name). AGENTS.md does not endorse this pattern; surrounding functions in the same files use accurate factory/verb names (makeAPIClient, initializeDatabaseManager), so a rename such as configureImageLoadingDefaults() would match local convention. Impact is limited: called once per target at bootstrap, no behavioral bug; a maintainer hunting for where image-request auth is set would be briefly misled, but it is a cosmetic clarity issue, so severity stays low.

Confirmed: SyncServer.swift streamChunk contains bare `try? handle.close()` at lines 531 and 543 with no AppLog call. AGENTS.md Logging Rules (line 203) categorically require every error-swallowing `try?` to log via AppLog.error/warning, so the pattern is explicitly forbidden rather than endorsed. The local convention also cuts against the pattern: SyncServer.swift logs errors thoroughly elsewhere (including in the same completion closure at line 542), and these are the only `try? .close()` occurrences in the repo. The suggested do/catch + AppLog.warning fix matches existing file style. Impact is minimal — close failure on a read-only handle is rare and harmless since the connection is cancelled regardless — so severity stays low.

Verified against the actual source. In /Users/qaq/Documents/GitHub/MuseAmp/MuseAmpDatabaseKit/Sources/MuseAmpDatabaseKit/DatabaseManager+Writes.swift, the @DatabaseActor wrappers `removeTrack(trackID:)` (lines 122-125) and `removeAlbum(albumID:)` (lines 139-142) exist exactly as described and only forward to `removeTrackSynchronously`/`removeAlbumSynchronously`. I attempted to refute by searching the entire repo (app target, tvOS target, package Sources and Tests) for any direct invocation: the only matches are the declarations themselves, the unrelated `MusicLibraryTrackRemovalService.removeTrack(trackID:)` method in the app target (which routes through `sendSynchronously(.removeTrack(...))`), and the `LibraryCommand.removeTrack/.removeAlbum` enum cases. The command dispatcher in DatabaseManager+Commands.swift (lines 18-23) calls the synchronous variants directly, and both wrappers are internal to the package, so no external caller can exist. The other @DatabaseActor methods in the same file (rebuildIndex, ingestAudioFile) are genuinely invoked from `send()`, so the dead wrappers are not part of a deliberate file-wide convention — they are leftovers. AGENTS.md contains no rule endorsing forwarding wrappers or requiring an actor-isolated variant per write method; its dependency/placement rules actually push callers toward the LibraryCommand path, which already covers async use. Deleting the two wrappers is safe and removes a misleading second entry point. Severity stays low: it is dead code that costs a few lines of reader confusion but has not misled any call site.

Verified: DownloadCoordinator.pauseAll()/resumeAll() (lines 42-48) contain only DBLog.info calls — no stateStore mutation, no event emission — while every sibling method (enqueue/retry/cancel) does real persisted work. The public LibraryCommand cases .pauseAllDownloads/.resumeAllDownloads (LibraryCommand.swift:19-20) route to them via DatabaseManager+Commands.swift:39-44 and return LibraryCommandResult.none exactly like genuinely effective commands, so the package API silently claims success. Repo-wide grep confirms no code ever sends these commands; real pause/resume is implemented independently in MuseAmp/Backend/Downloads/DownloadManager.swift:246,263 and invoked directly by DownloadsViewController. AGENTS.md does not endorse no-op command stubs; it documents the package as owning download state, which these stubs contradict. The suggested fix (remove the dead commands or implement the persisted status transition + downloadsChanged event) matches surrounding code style. Severity medium is appropriate: the API actively misleads any package consumer (e.g. the tvOS shell) into believing pause/resume works, but no caller currently exists so no bug has yet materialized.

Confirmed: clearTracks() is defined at MuseAmpDatabaseKit/Sources/MuseAmpDatabaseKit/Internal/IndexStore.swift:202 and a repo-wide grep finds no callers anywhere (app targets, packages, or tests) — only the definition. IndexStore is an internal type consumed by DatabaseManager/LibraryScanner, so this is not deliberately-reserved public library surface; sibling delete methods all have live callers, making clearTracks() an outlier rather than a convention. AGENTS.md does not mandate or endorse keeping unused store methods. Removing it is a genuine, convention-compatible clarity improvement. Severity remains low: it is dead surface that mildly inflates the reasoning burden about who can wipe the tracks table, but it misleads no one into a bug.

Confirmed: TVSessionStateAdapter.swift:688 advertises the Bonjour receiver service with a bare `port: 1` and no explanation, while the sender path (SyncTransferSession.swift ~177) advertises the real SyncServer listening port. The receiver advertisement is discovery-only — the iOS sender consumes only serviceName/deviceName/pairing code from discovered receivers and never dials their endpoint — so 1 is a deliberate dead placeholder, but nothing at the call site, in SyncBonjourAdvertiser, or in SyncConstants says so. SyncBonjourBrowser even builds SyncEndpoints from service.port for all roles, so port-1 endpoints flow into DiscoveredDevice, making the literal look like a potential bug to a reader. The fix (named constant in the existing SyncConstants enum in SyncProtocol.swift, which is already symlinked into the TV target, plus a one-line comment) matches the module's established convention of centralizing magic values (bonjourType, protocolVersion) and complies with the CLAUDE.md rule that comments are reserved for removing real ambiguity. No AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md rule endorses inline magic ports. Severity stays low: it is a single-line clarity issue that has not bred a bug because the receiver endpoint is never used for connection.
